ai_summary: Abilene Christian University (ACU) offers a close-knit , Christian environment where students often feel supported by both faculty and peers. Many reviews highlight the caring nature of professors , who frequently go out of their way to help students academically and personally, with smaller class sizes facilitating meaningful interactions. The campus itself is described as beautiful and welcoming , fostering a strong sense of community . However, there are consistent concerns about diversity and accessibility, along with critiques of the financial aid process and administrative support. Students particularly value the spiritual aspect of campus life , including mandatory chapel attendance, and appreciate the various opportunities for involvement through clubs and activities. While some find the atmosphere enriching and feel it’s a great fit for developing their faith, others express discomfort regarding the lack of diversity and inclusivity . Overall, ACU combines academic rigor with a focus on Christian values, though it may not suit everyone’s needs or expectations.

reviews.text: it’s been a great two years. I have have many ups and downs alongs the way because I was at a really low point in life when I decided to join ACU. I was dealing with my father’s sickness and while he was fighting for his life I decided to take works into action and after that I have not stopped. I started applying and looking for the best until I found it thank God! Acu allowed me to be able to work and have time for school, my peers are great students and it’s crazy to know they are all over Texas and how interesting to get to know them. Acu is like a big happy family, I have always felt very welcomed by every one. Sometimes I feel like there could be a little bit of exception on our deadlines especially for very busy students but other than that ACU is more than great! I was already familiar with Canva because I used it my sophomore year in high school and as well very quite easy to use. The type of assignment I recordé are very fun to make and very outgoing!

I love it.
Parties everyweekend
Chic fil a is good
People are nice
It’s awesome bc it feels as if I belong.

I absolutely love ACU. It has been the best school I could ask for, the professors are amazing and I have loved learning. I have made the best of friends and felt supported along the way by staff. There is tons of fun campus activities to do and Abilene is a great town.

I loved my time at ACU, my department was great and so were all of the friends that I made throughout. I still talk to everyone, including my professors. I did not find classmates and colleagues there, I found family.

My overall experience at Abilene Christian University has been a positive and rewarding. I've appreciated the supportive learning environment and the dedication of the instructors who truly care about their students' success. The courses have challenged me to think critically and apply what I have learned to real life situations. especially in my field of study. I also value the flexibility of online learning format, which allowed me to balance school and work, baseball for my son and family responsibility.

I have loved my time at Abilene Christian University so far! The campus is gorgeous, the social environment is positive and uplifting, and the centered nature in all aspects of the university is very uplifting. I cannot wait to see what else it has in store for me.

I reached out to an advisor and they were very attentive and helpful. I was provided a lot of resources and accommodations. As a mother I needed online to available for working at a school and being there for my children.
Not too bad, there just isn't really much to do when not in class both on campus (when clubs aren't meeting, and in the city).
Abilene Christian University is the best school ever! The admissions process was smooth, and everyone I spoke to was very welcoming.

My overall experience with Abilene Christian University is in great standing. The professors and staff members are amazingly nice, patient, and great educators. The opportunities that we are offered at ACU is definitely what stands out against other colleges. We have great traditions, free fun events, and so many career fairs to help us experience and build further foundation in our fields. They are the school that once you step foot and experience life there, you want to keep going back to build more memories. Although ACU is great, the only con of it all is the finances. Amongst all the fun and education, I still have the worry of my tuition payment in the back of my mind. They offer scholarships but not enough to where a lot of people won’t be in debt or worry about submitting their next payment.
It is a really nice college. I really like how there are not that many students here and how you can talk with your professors and also talk with the president of the school.

I loved Abilene Christian University! The campus is beautiful and so peaceful. All of the living accommodations are also very luxurious compared to other Universities. The environment of the campus is so fun and outgoing while also pushing students academically. I had always been in upper level classes throughout high school and my academic continuation at this university has continued to challenge me and grow me. Overall, ACU is a very fun school while maintaining a very high standard of academics. They do an amazing job of making students feel like they belong with all of the events and clubs they have to offer.

I had a great time at ACU. My favorite part of my entire experience was being a part of small groups on campus. I also loved getting involved in homecoming and wildcat week. I loved the christ-centered experienced as well

nothing at all. the experience at the school has been an easy transition to the online platform and my dedication to learning is definitely a plus as well as the support system from my student success advisors.
I liked the Christian environment of the school, however, the social environment felt a lot like high school.

ai_summary: At Adelphi University, students highlight the beautiful campus and supportive professors as standout features of their experience. The sense of community is strong, with many students noting the advantages of small class sizes that allow for personal interactions with faculty. However, there are significant concerns regarding the quality of food, housing conditions, and the treatment of commuter students, with some finding it hard to engage socially. Academic experiences vary, particularly in terms of departmental quality; while many praise the nursing and psychology programs, others express dissatisfaction with certain courses and faculty attitudes. The university is perceived as expensive, prompting calls for improved financial aid and better dining options. Overall, experiences at Adelphi appear to be a mix of positive academic support and community engagement, overshadowed by frustrations over administrative issues and campus life.

reviews.text: I have applied for the fall semseter. I visted the campus about 2 times already. The local area is very nice. The students on the campus are very welcoming along with the professors.

I'm a Sophomore at Adelphi University, and my overall experience has been great so far. I love the small classes and supportive faculty, who help me truly connect with the Professors, students, and faculty, and create connections. There is also a strong bond among students from different cultures and backgrounds, which is really important to me, as it makes me feel at home and helps me learn about different cultures. The campus has a very safe environment, and we get regular safety updates from public safety. Overall, I am truly grateful to be able to be a part of the community.

I enjoyed the lessons both in and out of the classroom. Many professors also go above and beyond and actually show a passion for their career and ways for us to be better or do things that help foster our growth instead. Our peofessors and classmates can often be our biggest supporters, especially when it comes to our Academia. I've made great friends and grew more as a person from the supportive and constructive environments. This place showed me how college is more than just class and exams, that I can also enjoy it and have that Panther-iffic School Pride!! Go Panthers!!

Adelphi as a college is very much a commuter school. What I mean by that is in comparison to many other colleges, even those on Long Island, Adelphi has a lot of students who come to class and then immediately leave campus. While in class, students will have an incredible time. All the professors are amazing and know their subjects and the lessons are extremely informational and thought-provoking. Outside of classes, students will need to work to find friends and colleagues who also choose to stay on campus Clubs can range anywhere in size from a good portion of a major to just the eboard, and while Adelphi puts on many incredible events, they can often feel barren of student engagement. The school works its hardest to give students a positive experience academically and socially, but it is worth being aware that many students on campus are counting down the minutes until class ends and they can take the bus to work or back home.

It’s really nice here. Very safe and welcoming and I really appreciate the academics. Sometimes campus can be a little dead especially on weekends since there are a lot of commuters however if you find a nice group of friends you’ll have a great time.

My experience at Adelphi University has been positive overall. I appreciate the supportive professors, small class sizes, and strong sense of community. I’d love to see more course options and campus events, but overall it’s been a welcoming and encouraging place to learn.

I am apart of Greek Life and met some of my lifelong friends at Adelphi. Although it paid off in terms of social life, I admit that the academics, especially in the College of Business, is lackluster. The campus is also heavily commuter based, so everyone leaves after 5-6pm during the weekdays. If you aren't in Greek Life, you won't experience the party scene to its true potential. The school is also getting very expensive, so it is wise to choose elsewhere if you aren't coming here on a lot of scholarship/for nursing.

My experience at Adelphi University has been amazing so far since when I transferred here this past fall. I was able to make friends quickly, get fully immersed into the school tradition without feeling out of place, and I was able to work with professors who truly cared about my academic success.
I love it here! Student life can get a bit dry since the school is a commuter based school, but there are still many clubs to join and opportunities to be had

Adelphi University provides numerous resources for scholarships, tutoring, and internship programs, while also providing a sense of community that larger schools can't accommodate for. Adelphi is very flexible in customizing my class schedule, so I have my required classes while also allowing me to sign up for the courses I'm passionate about.

As a first year student who has recently completed my first semester of college I can proudly say I know I made the right decision coming to Adelphi! I really enjoy the classroom structure and it’s part of the reason I came here. In comparison to a 200 student lecture at larger universities, Adelphi’s classes are intimate and you never feel like you are just a number. Additionally, the close knit community that is fostered here ensures you never go a day without seeing a familiar face. The Adelphi campus in general is really beautiful especially in the Spring and Summer months. Seeing the greenery, blooming flowers, and the bunnies are my favorite! If I could change anything it would probably be to have more student activities on campus during the weekends. Many of my out of state friends have no where to go on weekends and it can become redundant doing nothing. However, that’s concern is an easy fix! Overall, I love coming to AU and wouldn’t change my decision.

I love Adelphi so much. As a stem major, the community is so welcoming. There’s always a professor that is available to help you, a classmate that wants to work with you, and so many opportunities for internships and jobs. As someone who wants to be a teacher, there’s so many avenues that I’ve since learned that I’m able to take because of my professors being willing to help me get the career that I want. Also, as someone who is a commuter student who works on campus the job market on campus is really wonderful and the pay is also really wonderful. As someone who also doesn’t drive getting to campus and from campus is not as hard as I thought it would be, the shuttles are an availability for commuter students, and the buses and trains are also really nearby!

Professors are great! Love the classrooms and tutoring center! Counselors could be more specific and better. I always feel like I leave with more questions than I entered with. Party scene and greek life are great, but only if you know people in frats/sororities or are in one. Campus life is harder as an out of state student since most students commute. Campus is still beautiful and there's always something to do!

My experience at Adelphi University has been incredibly rewarding both academically and personally. Moving from Sweden to the U.S. was a big step, but Adelphi made the transition feel welcoming and manageable from day one.
During my undergraduate years, I built a strong academic foundation while competing on the men’s soccer team, where I eventually served as captain. Balancing athletics and academics taught me discipline, leadership, and time management. Small class sizes and supportive professors made learning personal and engaging.
My graduate experience in the MS in Business Analytics program challenged me in the best way. The coursework was rigorous and practical, with professors consistently connecting theory to real-world applications.
Adelphi was extremely supportive and accessible. From international services to academic advising, I always felt guided and encouraged. Adelphi gave me not just degrees, but confidence, opportunity, and a strong sense of belonging.

My experience with Adelphi University has been very positive.
What I value most is that Adelphi gives international students a real chance to start their education, even if they do not fully meet all requirements at first. My IELTS score was 5.5, and I was rejected by Boston University, but Adelphi offered me a six-month Accelerator Program. During this time, I improved my English while earning some academic credits, which helped me smoothly adjust to university life.
As a 38-year-old international student, the transition was not easy due to cultural and language barriers. However, the professors were always respectful, supportive, and understanding. My background in business also helped me succeed academically.
Today, I feel confident communicating in English and grateful to Adelphi for this opportunity. The campus is beautiful, with a gym, swimming pool, affordable dining, many events, and a calm environment. Manhattan is only about 40 minutes away.

ai_summary: At Alabama State University, students often describe a strong sense of community and support among peers and faculty, making the campus feel like a home away from home. Reviewers praise the friendly interactions and engagement from professors, highlighting the strong focus on student success and personalized attention . However, concerns do arise regarding the condition of campus facilities, particularly dormitories and dining options, with many wishing for improvements in maintenance and food quality. While students appreciate the vibrant campus life , including events and sports, they express a desire for greater diversity and inclusion among the student body. Overall, ASU is recognized for its rich cultural environment as a Historically Black College and University (HBCU), although there are calls for improvements in student services and housing to enhance the overall experience.

reviews.text: Alabama State University is a wonderful institution with strong academic priorities. However, I believe there is room for improvement in housing and overall campus security. Due to the university’s close proximity to the surrounding community, non-students are sometimes able to access and walk through campus.

My overall experience while attending Alabama State University was overwhelmingly positive. It shaped me into the person I am today, and I wouldn’t trade the lessons learned, the friendships made, or the pride of being a Hornet for anything. Every thing about the school is great from campus life to education. One thing i know that Hornets spirit is real. Only improvement i would consider is a more consistent and reliable Wi-Fi network across campus would also be a huge improvement for students

My overall experience for my first year at Alabama state university as a sophomore transfer student wasn’t bad. Kind of what I expected coming as a transfer, didn’t have much friends but the people there still spoke to me. Everyone is kind of respectful and nobody is really stuck up. I do recommend picking this school.

I love Alabama State university because it provides a community for you once you leave home. Attending Alabama state is like meeting a whole bunch of chosen family that you didn’t grow up with. Alabama State is a pretty good size school, but not too big to the point where you don’t know a lot of people. I also love that there are so many opportunities for you to join things or even start new things. To top everything off Alabama State is always trying to give scholarships, present new opportunities, and always present great new experiences! I have a lot of allergies so I wish the cafeteria would have more options for that that are actually good. And I wish laundry was free instead of having to pay. I also want some of the staff (mostly the cafe and custodial staff) to stop smoking cigarettes on campus, I have asthma and it’s just not a good look.

It’s a good college, theres just a couple things they should be up front about to new students. They automatically give you a certain scholarship even if you’re eligible for a better one and once you have one scholarship you can’t get another one. Also i’ve heard the cafeteria food has given students food poisoning my freshmen and sophomore year

It is a good school. Athletics are great. Everyone likes to have fun. The environment is good. The cafe is open at certain times for each meal. The classes are good some are a little challenging. Many different types of professors. The HBCU experience ! Many resources! The staff is willing to help. There is a lot of history on campus. As well as many great opportunities to give back to the community and ones in need. They have many international students. There is many degrees and programs that you can involve yourself into as well as want best for your future. Many alumni's give back to the school for the school to invest in better things and ways to improve the school. They also have on campus police and their stations is right by the school in case of any emergencies. They give many scholarships opportunities and have many contacts to give you if you are in need of help with money. They even have a program for first generation students. They are working on more dorms as well!

Alabama State University offers a decent academic environment with good faculty support. Coming into Alabama State University with a strong academic background and a quiet personality, I was pleasantly surprised by how welcoming and supportive the campus community was. Professors genuinely cared about student success. Most were were approachable, attentive, and easy to reach outside of class.
Academically, I appreciated the diversity of programs, especially in STEM. The university’s commitment to bloom leadership and personal growth is appreciated. Some things I would love to see change is the condition of some campus building, as well as some campus staff attitudes. Dorms and dining halls, while functional, could benefit from upgrades in cleanliness, maintenance, and food variety.

I'm a first-year student at Alabama State University. I wanted to write a review to inform prospective students or anyone who might be thinking about applying that this is a wonderful school. Class numbers are small, which makes it easier to interact with your teachers, the dining hall meals are great, the students are kind, you get unlimited food swipes, and there is a party almost every weekend. The campus is small, so you never have to walk far to class. I do enjoy the suite-style housing, even if the majority of students gripe about it. It's much better than shared restrooms. All dorms on campus are same-sex, with the exception of the apartments. I will be transferring my next year for a few reasons. I found it hard to make friends after a while. Although the school is located in Montgomery, a fairly large city, there's nothing to do within walking distance, you must drive to go anywhere good. My experience here was nice. It wasn't insanely good and it wasn't horrific.

As a freshman at Alabama State University, I can honestly say I love my HBCU experience so far! From the moment I arrived, I’ve been welcomed by friendly faces and a supportive community. I've enjoyed meeting new people, making connections, and being part of such a rich and proud tradition. The campus environment, the events, the culture—it’s all been amazing. My stay has been wonderful, and I wouldn’t change a thing. I’m proud to be a Hornet and excited for what’s ahead!

Alabama State University (ASU) has made significant strides in recent years, earning national recognition for its academic programs, affordability, and student success. In 2025, ASU was named one of the Colleges of Distinction, a recognition given to only 11 HBCUs nationwide, highlighting its commitment to engaged teaching, vibrant community life, and strong student outcomes. Despite these successes, ASU faces areas where improvement is needed. Some students and online commenters have expressed concerns about safety near campus, suggesting a need for improved security and more transparent communication. Additionally, while ASU does have some research centers, its overall research presence is limited compared to peer institutions, and expanding graduate programs and research initiatives could enhance its academic reputation.

As a freshmen at alabama state university from my experience.So far it has been elite as a first generation college student , from a single parent household.I have the best resource within tutors helping.The presidents and professors are the most supportive, parties we have on campus are friendly and fun.If I had i to change anything it would be the communication.

My experience at Alabama State University has been transformative. As a Forensic Chemistry major on the Dean's List, I received excellent academic support, with professors who truly care about student success. Balancing my studies with being a Stingette, a member of the NAACP, Tau Beta Sigma, and NSLS allowed me to grow both academically and as a leader. These organizations gave me opportunities to serve, perform, and develop skills that will stay with me forever.
However, I do think ASU could improve in certain areas. The STEM programs, particularly in chemistry, would benefit from updated labs and technology. Additionally, student organizations often lack funding, which makes it harder to fully realize their potential. More support for these groups would make a big difference.
Overall, my time at ASU has been invaluable, but increased resources for STEM and student organizations would enhance the experience for future students.

College life has been a journey full of growth, challenges, and new experiences. One thing I’ve really enjoyed is the freedom to explore who I am and what I want to do in life. Being around other students who are motivated and focused has pushed me to stay on top of my goals, both academically and personally. I’ve also appreciated the sense of community, especially at an HBCU where the culture, support, and energy are unmatched.
That said, college isn’t always easy. Balancing classes, work, and personal responsibilities takes a lot of discipline. One thing I’d like to see improve is more support for students who are juggling school and jobs.
Overall, college life has helped shape me into a more responsible, focused, and driven person. I’m proud of how far I’ve come and excited to see where this path leads me next.
Better parking. More student housing. Better communication from housing, financial aid, professors, etc.

As a student at Alabama State University, I have encountered many people from all around the country and some who are from other countries. The staff is willing to assist with any issues that you may have. I like that the campus is small and that ASU has taken some steps in bringing the campus into the 21st century. I would like for Alabama State University to incorporate more technical aspects to their degrees such as certifications and licensure for some of the majors that require them for work. In addition, more internship and/or job placement opportunities. Preparing students while in undergrad for the workforce will help to booster alumni support (participation and funding). I would like for ASU to implement more counseling/therapeutic services. Many of the students who attend are great academically, however emotional and personal issues tend to disrupt their academic achievement. These types of support services could increase ASUs retention and matriculation/completion rate.

Great experience, professional staff and well educated. Courses online and in campus are flexible and accommodate my working schedule. Albizu is a great university and the staff is from diverse cultures. The class rooms are well equipped and provide an atmosphere of good communication and support.

As a student at Albizu University, I can confidently say that it's a great place to learn. The professors are knowledgeable and passionate, and the coursework is challenging but rewarding. AU is a very family oriented school and there's a strong sense of community among the students. Overall, I highly recommend Albizu University to anyone looking for a top-notch education.
While the information taught was sufficient and well-delivered, I feel as though I was somewhat misguided during my time at Albizu.

This is the worst college or university I have attended. I was in the m Masters program before I was forced to drop out. Because I’ll be zoo with not offer classes I needed to complete my degree in a timely manner due to not replacing a professor who quit a year ago. The staff is very unhelpful and they do not try to help you navigate any of the registration or career coaching.
The classes are poorly structured and black real meaningful information. I would never go back.

Not a genuine place at all - check out how many students pass the state exam for psychology, mental health, etc .. you will see how much integrity this institution lacks, it says a lot regarding the type of training they provide. There are also no opportunities or networking a D school overall. The academic experience is low - they pretend they care, but they don’t - the curriculum is old and they think they are the best - some professors are Arrogant- they just pretend to be good but just stay away. No campus safety, no student life no cafeteria - sound good enough ?

They treat you like a number not a person. They dont comunicate anything about scholarships or tuition guides. Only they are interested to get you on finantial aid by the way the most expensive and the classes take up to 4 months when other universities take up to 2 months per class. In my case I spent 2 years studying and they decide to dismiss me without any intention to help.... the feedback for evaluations very poor non specific. They apply policies for their convenience no yours. The GPA is 3.00 you have to reach it but they dont apply the policy to give you the grade before 10 days you deliver your research papers. One more, they keep your originals papers even when you are dismissed, why? I dont have the minimum idea. They make you waste time and money to borrow... awful! Its incredible!

Small intimate classes make for a more one-on-one learning experience. Professors vary in experience and knowledge. Some classes very easy (almost too easy) and some classes are more difficult. Good professors are far and in between, but this is a great university for people who find is especially hard to do well in school. At least in the undergrad level, the classes are relatively easy to pass. Also great if you want to pursue career in Psychology or Mental Health industry. However, if you want to study anything else, probably not the best choice for you. No gym, no sports, no fun events. Community involvement is lacking.
School did a very nice clean up on the Psychology area.
There are not a lot of options at the school cafeteria. You have your basic sandwich, soups and salads. Also a couple of other treats available such as empanadas, pastelitos, and chips.
There are a variety of personalities at this school. Since the masters program takes place in the evenings, most people are in work wear.
The school is mostly surrounded by businesses/warehouses. There are a couple of apartments and homes near by.
There really is no party scene at this institution. The university is too small.
Campus overall is pretty safe. There is only one security guard that I have seen at the main entrance of the campus. There's no one covering the back side.

There is plenty to do in the Miami area. There are two malls within the vicinity of the school. You also have a couple of parks, you have the county fair in the spring team in Tamiami park. If you want something more exciting, you can hop into your car and drive to Brickell or South Beach for great restaurants, beach, activities.
There are students from all over attending classes at this campus. Anywhere from Haiti, Cuba Colombia, Jamaica, etc.
I'm about to start my first semester, but the grad students I've been in touch with all say glowing things and the communication from faculty and staff has been great so far.

Graduated from the PsyD program in 2006. I really enjoyed my time at the San Diego campus. The professors and courses were outstanding. The practicum and internship options were competitive with the large state and private universities in SD. AIU students were highly sought after and got top notch internships. Highly accredited (APA, WASC, etc), so it was easy to get jobs post graduation. Loved the huge emphasis on diversity within the curriculum of every class (mandated). Small class sizes!!! I felt very prepared for post-doc work. The campus was small, green, and safe, but needed some updates at the time to classrooms. Off-campus housing was expensive, but beautiful and safe. Great library, especially student access to online databases. Profs were available. Course content was superb & challenging. Class discussions were excellent. The profs were all practicing clinicians! So much insight into clinical work! Loved my experience (MA & PsyD).

I enjoy the flexibility of Alliant International University's online program, which allows me to balance my education with my personal and professional commitments. The self-paced structure enables me to manage my workload effectively and focus on topics most relevant to my interests. The diversity of students and discussion perspectives also adds a rich layer to the learning experience.
However, I'm not too fond of the lack of live lectures, which can make the program feel isolating and less interactive. The heavy reliance on reading for all learning makes it challenging to engage deeply with the material without additional explanation or instructor examples. I miss the opportunity for real-time interaction and clarification that traditional lectures provide, as well as the dynamic exchange of ideas in a live classroom setting.

ai_summary: Alvernia University is highlighted in reviews for its supportive academic environment and dedicated professors who genuinely care about students' success. Many students appreciate the small class sizes , which foster personal connections and allow for more interaction with faculty. The campus is described as beautiful and easy to navigate, contributing to a sense of community among students. On the downside, some reviews mention that the food options could be improved and that campus life can feel limited, especially on weekends when many students go home. There are also comments about the high tuition costs and some challenges regarding diversity and student activities. Overall, Alvernia University offers a nurturing atmosphere with ample opportunities for personal and academic growth, but potential students should consider their priorities regarding campus social life and dining options.

reviews.text: I have loved my time at Alvernia, the ability to get my Bachelors asynchronously has been a life changing experience. I have been incredibly successful in my classes and able to manage my personal and work life on top of being a full time student.
I love Alvernia. It is a small school that I was looking for. It doesn't have a big party scene either and was also why I chose it. I feel very safe and everyone is very friendly.
My experience with the administration has been very positive. Helpful and knowledgable. My advisor has gone above and beyond for me, as well as assisting me with my FFSA application.

I’m so glad I chose Alvernia to major in nursing. Small class sizes have made my studies easier. Professors know my name and who I am. The academic success center provides great tutors who helped me study and reinforced what I was learning which led to passing my classes in freshman year. Student Center food is ok but the campus offers other options that are better. Dorms are clean and comfortable. Lots of activities like trips and game show type games for students to participate in.

My overall experience at Alvernia University, has been so far okay. I feel personally as some professors simply had favoritism and were more in depth with students who they personally had a good connection with. As nursing program is already hard as is, it’d be nice to have more of a nursing community rather than one that no one is helping one another.

My experience has been both inspiring and transformative. I appreciated the support that some Professors gave me and even if it was hard at first I got the hang of it. The process wasn’t alway clear and accessible, which I found very hard because in high school teachers are so fast at explaining everything you have to do unlike some Professors. One aspect I liked was the community connection because I knew people from my high school but we weren’t friends in high school but became good friends by helping each other out in our first college year. However, I would like to see more Professors actually wanting to help students then just give us an assignment and that’s all.

I love Alvernia. I am junior, majoring in criminal justice with a minor in pre-law. I have a wonderful experience. People are extremely welcoming and friendly. My CJ professors are fantastic and I feel that they truly care for me and my overall success. I am involved in various opportunities around the campus, so if you want to get busy, there are options for you. I have made friends for life here and think Alvernia was the best choice for me. I hope to start our Police Academy this fall and then start applying the knowledge in practice.
It is a very peaceful campus. There might be times you feel alone, you will have to reach out and try to talk to people. Everyone is pretty welcoming so there is no need to be nervous.

Completed my practicum and internship there for clinical counseling. Glad I was able to and they allowed me as an exception. Wish I would have gone there 2009 instead of where I went (which was online). I would have retained more I believe.
This is a great college to go to. I am going for nursing. Professors are we easy to work with. Would recommend this college for everyone!

As a nursing student I had a challenging, but overall nice experience. During my freshman year I went through a variety of anatomy, chemistry, and microbiology courses where I explored a lot of interesting topics and built a strong foundation for future courses. Additionally, there were a lot of exiting activities and clubs going on around campus which led to new friendships and made my college experience enjoyable. Nevertheless, I do think that their career development office and navigators need to do a better job at orienting students who are in the process of switching majors. I was in this process myself during my second semester and got little to no guidance when I reached out to my advisor and the head of career development.
I love this college and my experience have been great so far. I am very glad with all the advices I have recital from my advisor.

So far, my experience at Alvernia has been fantastic. I am a freshman commuter, and even though I haven't been here for long, I am enjoying it immensely. I enjoy the events that are held here at Alvernia; there is so much to do. There are numerous opportunities at various events such as career fairs and more.

The people here couldn’t be better, the professors are some the most helpful people i have ever met, and are always willing to help. It feels like the staff genuinely wants you to succeed. However the administration can be hard to deal with. They are virtually impossible to get in contact with when you need help. At times it feels like the school is more worried about not spending money then making its students happy and healthy and in a position to succeed.

Everyone at Alvernia is extremely welcoming. The professors are always willing to help out and never let me down when I have a question, comment, or concern. The campus is safe, warm and welcoming. I call Alvernia my home during the day.

ai_summary: American International College (AIC) offers a unique experience characterized by small class sizes and a diverse student body, creating a close-knit academic community. Many students appreciate the attentive and caring faculty , who provide ample support and foster personal connections. While the academic programs, particularly in physical therapy and nursing, receive positive feedback for their quality, some students find the coursework less challenging than expected. Student life includes a variety of activities and events, but participation seems variable, with calls for a more vibrant campus culture . However, housing conditions and the surrounding area raise concerns, as many reviews cite issues like outdated dorms and safety worries . Additionally, while the school’s funding for athletics has been decreasing, the presence of strong sports teams remains a point of pride for some. Overall, AIC is seen as a supportive environment where students can thrive academically, but there is room for improvement in areas like campus facilities and safety.

reviews.text: There is a lot that has changed about the school in the 3 years that I have been here, but I believe the school is changing for the better and the people responsible are continuously looking for more opportunities to improve the campus.
I am transferring to AIC from another college . So I still need to experience AIC. I am looking forward to the Nursing program.

I am an incoming freshman student so I haven't had the opportunity to experience everything. I have been on campus multiple times and had the opportunity to spend the weekend on campus. I met many staff and students, and I love how everyone is so supportive and constantly checks in on you. I can't wait to start school there!

Over the past 4 years being in college, I can really say, from a commuter point of view, that student life is something very important to both students and staff. They have great food, plenty of activities planned throughout the week, diversity inclusivity events, and a strong passion to embrace the AIC way. And even though I am not the most active or participating college student, I am proud to be a Yellow Jacket.

American International College can be described as the perfect college for athletic student and international students. It creates a safe and welcoming environment for its students especially international students and tends to meet the needs of students on every level, while providing them with the best education experience possible. It has a beautiful campus and smaller classroom sizes as well.

When I visit American International College, It was the best I ever seen. The students on campus are so helpful and kind to one another. The food is amazing. The adults that works there, teacher, etc are super helpful too and want the best for you when you leave the school.

My overall experience at American International College has been positive. My biggest criticism is with the advising center. As a freshman and the first one in my family to go to college, I didn't understand how to transfer all of my college credits I earned in high school. It would have been really beneficial if my advisor had helped me through that process.

I went first arrived to American International College in 2021, it started fine but as I completed my undergraduate I realized I wish I had chosen a different school- I felt as if AIC academics weren't challenging me nearly enough, and it was mostly based around the athletics (which is why I chose to go there in the first place; I was on scholarship)but I wish it was a better balance of athletics, community, academics and a more prevalant campus culture.

Although I am a starting Freshmen at American International college I have noticed a significant difference in the academic life, it has been surprisingly too easy and hasn't challenged me. The area of this college is not the best but there is top security to make its students feel safer. The school has cut a few sports out such as wrestling and moved Hockey from D1 to D2. They have cut their post grad nursing program and a few accounting classes. The food here is decent all freshamn student meal plans comes with $300 towards the hive, Starbucks, Wingsover, and dominos which is something no other schools really have. Ultimately I have come to the decision that I will be transferring after two years because I want something more challenging.

During my first semester at AIC, I experienced numerous new things. I really liked the professors and the classes I was taking within the Physical Therapy program. The housing isn’t the best for first or second years, but it’s still a fun place to live. The only thing I would like to see change is the support of athletics, as many of our programs are losing funding.
American International College is a very good school. I would recommend people coming here. The health science majors are something i recommend.

I always knew American International College was the place for me. My sister attended AIC, played softball, and spoke highly of her experience, inspiring me to follow in her footsteps. Now, as a student, I’ve found my own path here and feel proud to call AIC my home. One of my favorite parts of campus life is my work-study job as a building assistant. I enjoy interacting with students daily, which helps me feel more connected to the community. Student life is vibrant and diverse, with plenty of opportunities to get involved. While the dorms foster a strong sense of community, I think the bathrooms could be improved, and I’d love to see better dining options on weekends to match the quality we enjoy during the week. Still, AIC has been a great experience overall. The close-knit campus and supportive environment have helped me grow academically and personally, and I’m excited to continue making the most of my time here.

I think AIC will be an amazing school to perdue my career in and further development my entrepreneurial aspects of my future… i am also apart of the athletic program and seeing how i’m constantly on the go i need a reliable source to keep all my information in check and AIC just doesn’t provide that.

American International College has been a real thrill so far and i’m having fun just being here. The classes are educating and i’m able to focus well here, counselor and staff are also very nice and helped me from the time of applying to now.

American international college is a college for all diverse students who wants to learn and become their very best at being successful. My overall experience is wonderful making me want to continue my education to be the best I can.

ai_summary: American University has a rich academic environment , especially noted for its strong programs in fields like international studies and political science, supported by knowledgeable professors who engage well with students. The campus is described as beautiful and well-maintained, offering an appealing suburban feel while being close to the opportunities of Washington, D.C. However, the experiences of students vary greatly, with some expressing dissatisfaction with the administration and student life, particularly regarding a perceived lack of school spirit and limited social opportunities beyond fraternity life. Many reviews highlight the campus's left-leaning political atmosphere , which can create an unwelcoming environment for those with differing views. Students also express concerns about the quality of dining services and housing conditions, particularly in freshman dorms. Despite these challenges, many appreciate the networking and internship opportunities available due to the campus's location in the nation's capital.

reviews.text: good university.had very good life .had very well environment and our university has very well educated professors
overall happy with the university, the location is great for opportunities in the city. very expensive but gives good scholarships. most pros have experience in field and knowledgeable.

American University is located in a wonderful part of Northwest DC where you can enjoy the suburban feel of campus while also being a short metro ride away from the heart of the most powerful city in the world. Professors are some of the best in their field and provide hands-on informative learning environments, and professional opportunities are endless. On the other hand, campus life is not the best, student involvement groups do their best to gather students and share experiences, but morale and school pride remains low around campus. American does some things great and guarantees you a high quality education, but socially, it suits more individualized students who want to attend and advance their careers and are less concerned about having fun.
Au is a very underated school! It's a medium size university in a large city, but he campus is a beautiful arboretum that provides safety and a traditional campus feel.
I demand the firing of Earl Wayne. He has an active warrant in Mexico to serve a life sentence for impregnating an 11 year old child.

I enjoyed my time at American University greatly. The campus is beautiful and eventful. American allowed me to meet so many amazing people! The degree program that I completed was rigorous, helping me learn so much about the field. The DC area is lively, and a great place to explore and discover new things.

American is a very diverse university’
in both academic fields and student backgrounds. The university lacks different political views and leans toward a specific political ideology that shows up in the policies as well as in the classroom. The environment is student led and strives on involvement
It’s been alright, it’s an okay colleges but tuition is insane for what it offers. Doesn’t necessarily make sense.

I absolutely love American! It’s definitely what you make of it. The social scene is not thrown in your face you need to seek out opportunities, but as long as you know the right people you will have no problem making friends, partying, and getting involved. All of the professors really do care about you and also being in D.C. is incredible. They give you a student bus pass and you also have easy access to public transportation it’s not as far as people make it seem. I like how I can have quad and a campus to learn, but also easy access to so many college students and the city.
I don’t want to sway people one way or another, but I had really great professors and classes. I also seriously recommend getting involved on campus

I'm majoring in international relations and wanting to find a job in government, AU certainly gave me a lot of opportunities to chase my dream. Keyword is my dream, if you are not planning to stay in DC or find a government job, this school is not really the best choice. This schools wants you to believe that its prestigious, and I mean its a good school dont get me wrong, but comparing this to Georgetown or even GW is honestly a night and day. The campus is a breathe of fresh air, school spirit is certainly lacking comparing to other privates, party scene is there but once you gone to one party you basically experienced all they have. Academic wise its good, not for this price but I'd say its one step above publics colleges, but not to ivy league or anything remotely close to that. The school shines in connection building however, as I said at the start, if you want to be part of government or DC related jobs, then consider this as decent option.
Overall, an American university I feel welcome. There’s a lot of connections in job opportunities, including internships. I made a few friends it’s easy to get to from Campus to my home
I like the CAS professors many of them are lots of fun and very engaging. There's so much to learn from them. I dislike the majority of professors in SPA as they are very racist.

AU is great considering the area it's located in--plenty of ways to gain easy access to jobs and networking opportunities. Student life itself is not so bad, it's your average college experience. I'd like to see more engagement in overall community.

I cannot say enough positive things about AU.
My years spent studying here were the best of my life.
The professors are outstanding, as are the student involvement opportunities.
Downtown Washington, D.C., is easily accessible by public transit from campus, and the university offers UPasses that cover Metro fares.
The student body at AU is motivated and kind. The dining, especially the student-run coffee shops, are great.
I am grateful for having committed to AU, and highly recommend it.

ai_summary: At Andrews University, students highlight the enriching blend of spiritual life and diverse cultural experiences, thanks to its global community. Many reviews commend the approachable and supportive professors who enhance the academic experience while fostering a close-knit environment, allowing for personal connections. The campus offers a serene atmosphere conducive to learning, though some students express concerns about outdated housing and limited student life activities. While the food and campus facilities receive mixed reviews, most agree on the strong emphasis on high academic standards and a commitment to holistic education . However, the high tuition costs and the administration's strict policies, especially regarding student freedoms, can be drawbacks, particularly for those not aligned with the university's religious focus. Despite some challenges, the campus community is described as welcoming, making it a unique place for personal growth and development.

reviews.text:
Being here at Andrews university has thought me faith. As an international student, getting finances is very hard but I know that through Christ, all things will be possible.
One of the things I love mostly is the diversity of the university. Persons from all walks of life attend this this university; something that I find very refreshing.
One thing I would love to see change is the increase of the amount finances paid to student workers especially in areas that requires intense brain worker or physical labour.
There are a lot of opportunities to be social but nothing is forced. There are some events that are wayyy too loud but that is a personal opinion.

I would highly recommend Andrews to anyone seeking a well-rounded, enriching college experience. What stands out most is its God-centered environment, where faith is encouraged and supported throughout campus life. The community is welcoming and friendly, making it easy to feel at home from the very beginning.
The university truly values diversity—both cultural and personal—which creates an atmosphere of mutual respect and learning. Students from many backgrounds come together, and that mix of perspectives adds so much to the academic and social experience.
Beyond the classroom, there’s a wide range of extracurricular activities that help students grow not only academically but also socially and spiritually. Whether it’s joining student organizations, cultural groups, or athletic programs, there are countless ways to get involved and build lasting connections.
Overall, Andrews is a place where education, faith, and community come together beautifully.

It has been a really good experience, everything it's really different from were I come from ( Guatemala). There's a lot of activities to make you be in confidence and make friends like family groups it's a really good idea. The food it's really good to and is interesting that everything it's vegeterian. The dorms are nice too I have a roommate and she is really nice with me. The professors are really kind and hey are very attentive to my academic needs

The university is staunchly Christian, so if that's what you're into then it might be a good fit for you.
Students who attend the university come from diverse backgrounds, so you are bound to run into a wide range of worldviews which is an essential part of college life.
I know there is a well-loved basketball scene, but that wasn't my area of interest so I can't speak more on it.
N stl d in a quiet rural setting with great faculty and staff. Academically challenging yet supportive academically. A Christian focused setting with great diversity and inclusion. A great school
I just graduated from Lake Nelson Adventist Academy and I'm ready to start a new chapter at Andrews...
It is a school where the environment is friendly but it is mainly for people who want to focus and work hard and not a type of relaxed school/ party school.
I liked that Andrew College is a very big but compact College, and the coaches are cool. And it seems OK.

As a student at Andrews University I enjoy the mix of people, hearing different languages, learning new ideas, and making friends from around the world. Even though the area is quiet sometimes, I feel good during deep talks and late-night study sessions that bring us together. There are times when I get frustrated. Maybe the dorms aren't great, or I want more fun events on weekends. But I know that Andrews is not just about getting a degree; it’s about growing, strengthening my faith, and making lasting friendships.

The cafeteria and on campus convenience store was very good. The individual academic departments for the most part are also fairly easy to work with. However the residence hall, specifically the girls dorm, Lamson Hall, is very outdated and sometimes hard to live in. The residence halls need major updates and renovations.

I attended Andrews University in 1990-1991 when my Dad was completing his MBA, Accounting Studies. I felt safe when he was around. Even after he left, I was not able to continue my schooling there, but stayed in the community. Went back in 1998-1999, then moved to New Jersey and have not been back. I missed the fellowship there. I learned a great deal about myself and how to thrive. I also learned about the snow and how to deal with seasonal affective disorders. Important to have good people around you.

Andrews university is a great university. I like how they can focus on each and every learner individually. I like how they make sure you grow academically and spiritually . I like how they provide healthy food and the rules there make sure every student develops good ethics and character . I also like how they provide financial assistance whether you work for it or you earn it.
It is an amazing place that nurtures your academic, spiritual, physical, and mental aspects of life.

Andrews University is a Seventh-Day Adventist Institution offering a variety of majors and minors of both undergraduate and graduate degrees. Food served by the University cafeteria is well above the average and professors are, by and large, congenial, informed, and effective. However, campus housing options are often in a state of disrepair, student life lacks an adequate number of events per semester, and University politics are in shambles.

ai_summary: At Arizona State University, the reviews highlight a vibrant campus culture characterized by immense school spirit and a variety of social activities. Many students appreciate the depth and engagement of the academic programs, particularly enjoying the diversity of coursework and the supportive faculty . However, experiences with the quality of professors can vary, with some being highly communicative and passionate while others are perceived as less engaged. Student life is enriched by numerous clubs and Greek organizations, although some students find particular cultures within these groups don't align with their values. While the campus offers a wealth of resources and opportunities, particularly for online learning and student support, challenges like costly tuition for out-of-state students and navigating a large, complex campus can present hurdles. Overall, ASU provides a unique blend of academic rigor and social engagement, making it a memorable experience for many students.

reviews.text: Arizona State University's online neuroscience course has been immensely helpful. The professors have all bee very helpful and understanding. They know that I am not a traditional student and have helped me when I need an extra hand. The course work is challenge in all the right ways and the program itself is well structured. Would definitely recommend.
Overall, the experience at ASU is pretty good. Met a couple of people and learning a lot during my time as a Computer science major.

Arizona State University is a very welcoming university with an encouraging and lively environment. I had the opportunity to participate in several clubs and meet many people from different nationalities. I am currently living in a dormitory that, honestly, is not one of the best because it is older, but I still plan to explore some of the newer dorms that have recently been renovated. The dinning halls serve a variety of food with acceptable quality, and people are generally very kind and welcoming. I notice that there are only a few main libraries on the Tempe campus. The two primary one are Hayden Library ad Noble Library. They are great places to study, although I think some improvements could be made to the furniture and infrastructure. Overall, I would definitely recommend ASU. The atmosphere is positive and inspiring for studying.

Personally, Arizona State University, has been a great academic experience so far. I am about to finish my first year at Arizona State and I can say I am extremely grateful of how welcoming this year has felt so far.

To be honest, I feel like I've found a second home at ASU. The excitement on campus is unparalleled, and I adore events like Inferno, Devilpalooza, football games, Friday pop-ups, and all the Tempe cafes. Everyday living is fascinating because of the cuisine, the people, and the general atmosphere. My own home is far away, but ASU has made it simple for me to fit in and feel like I belong. This is a friendly, varied community with a strong sense of school spirit. My time in college has been significant and unforgettable since I was a part of this environment.
I enjoyed participating in some club groups that I care about. I loved attending to some events. It's amazing of the resources they have supporting my accommodations.

In my personal transfer experience, this school's transfer system works completely differently from what's stated on the website and what I was told in advising sessions. For example, transfer applicants can be treated like freshmen with entirely different requirements. When I asked questions, I received automated emails that made me feel like a cash cow. If you're an international transfer applicant, proceed with caution before applying.
I really enjoy attending ASU. It's more expensive but I would argue a better experience than the community college I attended.

I've enjoyed it a lot so far. While it is a pretty large student population, I've never felt overwhelmed or cramped since there is actually enough buildings and spaces for students, so I've never had a hard time looking for somewhere to sit down. The only con is traffic sucks during peak hours and I'm a commuter.
I’ve really enjoyed going to ASU, I just started in January and I feel like a I belong. It was the perfect match for me !
ASU has a very flexible online program that fits my busy schedule. I recommend them to anyone I know considering pursuing higher education.

Overall, my experience with Arizona State University has been positive. I really like the wide range of academic programs, the flexibility with course formats (in-person, hybrid, and online), and the amount of resources available to students. The campus is lively, there are many opportunities to get involved, and the school puts a strong emphasis on innovation and career preparation. That said, I would like to see improvements in academic advising and class availability, as it can sometimes be difficult to get timely guidance or enroll in required courses. Tuition and fees are also expensive, especially for out-of-state students, so more financial support would make the experience even better overall.

I’ve enjoyed my experience at ASU when it comes to the people I’ve met, especially the professors. Most professors I’ve had, have always been extremely open when it comes to needing help or guidance within the classroom, which was something I was nervous about before transferring. If there could be anything I could change at ASU, it would be the structure of the Anatomy and Physiology course in downtown Phoenix. This class has been extremely difficult with very minimal exceptions to seek credit. A few other things I would change, would be more public announcements for any day of events so students can find something fun to do between classes. Lastly, I would change the way class registration is set up, it’s always a battle to make sure you’re in the courses you need to graduate and if not, you’re on a waitlist.
At the beginning the process is quite confusing but it gets easier and becomes second nature. So far I am enjoying my online classes for my degree.
CLAV GOT FRAME MOGGED THIS HELL HOLE SOMETHING NEEDS TO BE DONE ABOUT THIS ASAP, THIS IS THE SAME AS A DEATH IN MY FAMILY

ai_summary: At Arkansas State University, students generally appreciate the friendly and supportive environment , which fosters both academic and social growth. Many reviews highlight the quality of professors who actively engage with students, providing assistance and fostering relationships that contribute to a positive educational experience. The campus itself is described as welcoming and well-organized, with opportunities for involvement in various activities and student organizations. While there are accolades for the affordable tuition compared to other universities, some students express concerns about housing conditions and administrative responsiveness, particularly regarding the application and financial aid processes. The athletics and campus spirit are also noted as significant aspects of student life, although some wish for more weekend activities beyond sports. Overall, Arkansas State University offers a blend of personal attention from faculty and a vibrant campus life , making it a unique choice for students seeking a manageable college experience.

reviews.text: Wish the teachers would communicate more online…overall it has been challenging but it’s worth it in some cases
Overall, I enjoyed my ASU experience. There was a diverse amount of degree options and flexibility for online courses where needed.
Safe quiet environment. .

So far, it hasn't been bad. Some courses are taught well and are in-depth, but others may use outdated information or lack guidance. Most of the teachers are good, but there are a handful who are hard to reach and receive an email or call back from. Overall, though, A-State has a large variety of course options and community opportunities.

My overall experience at Arkansas State University has been really positive. One of the things I appreciate the most is how quiet and peaceful the campus feels. It’s a place where you can actually focus on your studies without a lot of chaos going on around you. The classrooms are usually calm, and the library is one of my favorite spots because it’s perfect for getting work done.
I also like that the professors are generally helpful and willing to explain things when you’re confused. There are a lot of resources available, like tutoring centers and academic advising, which makes it easier to stay on track. The campus is clean and feels safe, which is important for students who are far from home.
If there’s anything I would like to see change, it would be more student events or activities, especially on weekends. Sometimes campus can feel a little too quiet, and having more things to do could help students meet new people and feel more connected.

So far, the experience has been wonderful. I commute to campus, so it has been quite challenging for me to make friends and engage in activities that are outside of the class requirements. However, there are many events and programs that happen if you actively seek them out.
I’m still within the registration process. However, so far they have been extremely helpful during the process.
It is a very pretty campus with a lot of things to do around the school and in the town of Jonesboro

My overall experience with Arkansas state has been a great experience so far I lovely campus life. I love to teach the professors and activities they have for us to do on campus. I like the cafe restaurants and food. I like all the activities they have for us to do when we’re bored I love the campus life living. I love my dorm is a pretty nice size. And I wouldn’t like to see anything changed. College is great so far.

My experience with Arkansas State University has been very positive so far. The enrollment process was smooth, and the staff has been helpful in answering my questions about financial aid and classes. Taking the Accuplacer test showed me where I need to improve, and I see the developmental math course as a chance to build a stronger foundation. The flexibility of the online program is a huge benefit since I am balancing work, family, and school. Even though I am just starting out, I feel supported and motivated to achieve my degree in Engineering Management Systems.

My experience at Arkansas State University has changed my life. I came from a very rural area with nothing to do, so it is a very big shift. The people here are great, and there are events going on at all times. The only thing I would like to see change is the meal plan requirements/cost. I do not believe it should be a requirement nor cost as much as it does.

I had a great experience while on campus. While an on campus student, I had great advisors. When coming back to school and being fully online, I did not have a great experience with advisors. All my professors were great. I would definitely recommend this college to others. I would like to see ASU improve their online degree programs.

What I really like about Arkansas State University in Jonesboro is the welcoming atmosphere. The campus feels big enough to have plenty of opportunities, but still small enough that you don’t feel lost in the crowd. The people are friendly, and it’s easy to make connections with professors, classmates, and student groups.
I love how there are so many different organizations and activities to get involved in, whether it’s Greek life, athletics, or campus events.
Another thing I appreciate is the balance between academics and community. A-State offers great programs in areas like nursing, business, and education, but it also focuses on giving back to Jonesboro and supporting the local area. The city itself is nice too—it’s not overwhelming, but it has enough to do when you want to go out with friends or explore.Overall, what I like most about Arkansas State is the sense of belonging, the opportunities to get involved, and the energy on campus that makes it feel like home.

I haven't been here for long, But I really like the way the campus itself is set up, it's very open but walkable.
I do wish some of the events were more time and travel forgiving as I have had to miss some events because classes were going on at the time.

Arkansas State University has the potential to be the best college around. In my earlier years at the college, they seemed amazing to me. I am currently pursuing my second bachelors degree here. The first one went very smooth and I had so much support! There are many, many professors and advisors who are amazing. However, the last year or so I have noticed the college is more worried about money and their own professors then their students. More and more students are being accused of cheating just simply for AI being created or any sort of social media, they advertise one price then there are other hidden fees posted to the student account that are part of the tuition. The college also needs to do better with the online aspect. 100% online is supposed to be for working adults, but it’s almost impossible to pass courses with a full time job.
To summarize, they offer so many wonderful degrees in campus and online, and have the potential to be amazing again! I look forward to graduating!

ai_summary: Auburn University is often described as a welcoming and community-oriented institution where students feel a strong sense of belonging . The campus itself is praised for its beauty and vibrant atmosphere , particularly during sports events. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty who are dedicated to student success, although some mention variability in teaching quality among professors. Students appreciate the numerous opportunities for involvement in extracurricular activities and organizations, which contribute to a lively social scene . However, there are concerns regarding the university's cost of attendance, and some students call for greater diversity within the student body. Overall, Auburn University stands out for its combination of academic rigor, spirited campus life, and a close-knit community feel.

reviews.text: I love attending Auburn! I could not see myself going anywhere else to complete my degree. The town has everything to offer for daily life, as well as the campus. Auburn truly is a family and nowhere else makes you feel as seen and important in your personal education as Auburn does! So many opportunities to further your education and love where you go to school!

I have had the best time at Auburn so far, it is the best environment with the best people. The faculty are super supportive. However, the housing situation is a little rough, on campus housing is expensive and hard to come by, and off campus housing has varying degrees of quality and affordability.

My time at Auburn University has been one of the most transformative seasons of my life. Auburn challenged me academically while surrounding me with a community that constantly pushed me to grow. Through my studies in biological sciences, serving as an Undergraduate Teaching Assistant, and involvement in various organizations, I developed not only a stronger academic foundation but also leadership skills and confidence in my calling to pursue medicine.
Beyond the classroom, Auburn became a place where I built meaningful relationships, embraced service, and learned the value of balance and hard work. Auburn shaped me into a more compassionate, driven, and grounded individual, and for that, I will always be grateful.

During my time at Auburn University, I thoroughly enjoyed myself. I started as a Nursing major, joined a sorority, and was a part of the club soccer team. After my first semester, my advisor helped me change majors to Microbiology Pre-PA because we determined it’d be a better fit. The academic staff supported my learning through tutoring opportunities and SI sessions. Without the academic support, my experience would have been much more difficult. In my sorority, I was supported as well through the academic aids in the chapter. I also got the opportunity to be immersed in the Greek community, helping me build my social circles and have a full and satisfying social experience. The campus is very walkable, which I enjoyed, but there was also the option to ride the university buses around campus and I definitely utilized them regularly. I felt safe and supported through my journey as a student and I wouldn’t change a thing.

I had a good time taking online course at Auburn. The professor made it really easy on canva to see and do all the work you needed to do. Anytime I emailed them they responded quickly and helped me out.
Amazing. Heavy on the southern culture, so if that’s what you want then Auburn is great. Not a crazy amount of things to do around the city though.

Auburn is a wonderful school that made me feel right at home. I’m currently attending, and I’m challenged in classes but they’re doable and the campus life and surrounding areas are packed with fun for everyone.

Auburn is such an amazing place. The campus, the people, the professors, the town, every thing about this place has so much to offer. I truly feel like I am getting an amazing education, all while being surrounded by the best people who all love this place.

My Auburn experience has been absolutely amazing so far. I have met so many kind individuals that I can call my friends. Everyone is so nice and welcoming and I can't say this about every single professor but almost all of them are rooting for your success and want you to do well in their classes. I like everything about Auburn and I'm not sure there's anything that I would change.
I love Auburn!! From out of state and I love it here. Campus is beautiful, social scene is great, academics are great too.

My overall experience at Auburn University has been very positive. I appreciate the strong sense of community and school pride, which makes it easy to feel connected both inside and outside the classroom. The campus is welcoming, well maintained, and has a supportive atmosphere that encourages student involvement. I have also found many professors to be knowledgeable and approachable, especially when it comes to helping students understand course material and apply it in practical ways.
Academically, Auburn offers solid resources and opportunities, particularly in engineering, and I value the emphasis on hands-on learning and real world applications. Outside of academics, there are plenty of ways to get involved, whether through organizations, campus events, or recreational facilities.
I was a transfer student at auburn. i enjoyed the campus life and never felt in danger ! the food is good too!

I loved my time at Auburn. The community and student body have a way of making you feel like you live in a small town. Making friends is pretty easy, so getting involved in campus events is also pretty fun. The students are typically nice, and the campus as a whole really embraces the "Auburn family" mentality. The campus is beautiful, downtown has a good selection of restaurants, and the gym is amazing. The student section during football season really comes to life, there's not a whole lot I can compare it to, so I would say you just have to experience it.
Some things I would like to see change:
-value all students not just the business or cosam ones
-fix parking!!!

I have loved being a Freshman at Auburn. The student involvement has been great, I had alot of friends going to Auburn I knew but none are in my major. I have met so many amazing friends. I did not rush and everyone is still so inviting and nice to me. I am majoring in Agricultural Science and I am so happy with it.
Absolute best decision I've ever made and I can't imagine being anywhere else! The campus, people, courses, and everything in between is all you can ever ask for and more.

ai_summary: Reviews of Augusta University present a mixed but generally favorable picture for prospective students. Academically, the professors are frequently described as approachable and supportive , fostering a close-knit environment where students feel recognized rather than just a number in a large class. Many students highlight the university's focus on health sciences , suggesting that it excels in programs such as nursing and offers strong connections to local healthcare facilities for practical experience. The campus environment is noted for its diversity and welcoming atmosphere , although some students feel that campus life and social events could be improved, as many students are commuters. The campus itself is praised for being clean and well-maintained, although there are concerns about housing conditions and food quality. Tuition is seen as affordable, offering a good value for the quality of education provided. On the downside, some administrative processes could be streamlined for better efficiency. Overall, many students appreciate the supportive community at Augusta University, especially for those pursuing careers in health disciplines, but the social scene may not appeal to everyone.

reviews.text: I love Augusta University, and especially the professors. The professors truly want to see you pass, and help you excel. The campus is so beautiful and underrated in my opinion. The libraries are pretty nice, and the food is good too.
Great school, cool professors , though much of college life isn’t all there. The city is growing and hopefully the lifestyle there will increase along with it
Augusta University has a lot of opportunities to meet new people, explore new things, and create great memories. The people you can meet here at AU are people that will be in your life forever.

Overall, I had a really good time at Augusta University. My Psychology professors were amazing for the most part and I got to take some really interesting classes. There were a lot of events and chances to get t-shirts, free stuff, and socialize with others. I also had the chance to work in the Student Life and Engagement office and it was really eye-opening to see how much work went into putting on events for students. It has been a few years, so I am not sure how it is there now. I know it was a little smaller when I first started. I would have liked more healthy and versatile meal options on campus.
