My experience at Augusta University has been mostly positive since I started back in Fall of 2024. I've made some great friends and met some really assume professors. I have joined many clubs and orgs such as, Black women in medicine, Knitted Knockers, and the Multicultural Mentorship Program, which keeps me really engaged on campus. I will say I love how diverse the campus is and how engaged they are with their students. However while they do take care of us and keep us included, there lack of attention to the lack of housing has caused issues with many students across campus.
I like the campus and the surrounding areas. Professor and nice and willing to work with you. overall a good college choice
I am a local to the area, however even being at an Augusta university still gives me a college campus feel. I do not regret going to school locally.

The professors at Augusta University are amazing and understanding of issues students may face on a daily basis. Many have open office hours to review course material or will refer students to the Academic Success Center if they notice low grades on recent assignments. I do want to see more life on campus to help us as students stay motivated and cheerful as we continue through the semester.

One of the things I love most about Augusta University is the strong community among students and professors. Even though my journey here has had challenges, I’ve felt supported by professors who want to see me succeed. I also value the opportunities Augusta provides for students in STEM, especially those of us pursuing degrees in biology and related fields.
That being said, I believe there are areas where Augusta could continue to improve to better support students. For example, more financial workshops and guidance around tuition deadlines, payment plans, and aid eligibility would be very helpful for students like me who are navigating higher education being a first generation student. Additionally, more resources for first-generation and immigrant students would ensure that we are not left behind simply because we face extra barriers.
Overall, I am proud to be a part of Augusta University.
It was really bad! August doesn't have any safety at all there campuses are open to the public and the sjuttles take forever and everyone is nice and sometimes clean

Admitly, my experience with Augusta University has been a pretty frustrating one. As the school seems to care more about their appearance than they do care for the actual student, with them prioritizing new flooring and getting funding to build a new research building over providing students with more dorms so they can have a place to stay. While majority of the professors here are great, it would be great to know that school actually cares for their students here especially since most students that attend come from out of town, state and even country.

Overall, it was a much better experience than the first university I attended. While there were some issues with paperwork, some professors, and not exactly the best area in town, it was still a good experience. There will always be the bad apple professors no matter where you attend, but I would recommend most of the professors that teach here.

Honestly, the stem department was very involved and had heavy rigor. I’m very proud to have worked alongside the wonderful stem professors they had. There was a variety of options in terms of food, the buildings were all very safe, and the lab buildings were immaculate.

It really feels like the peak college experience, but a lot safer and very diverse. The professors teach well, and it's evident that they genuinely love what they do. It's very different from high school; it feels like the community is genuinely there for you and will give you support when you need it.

Well, Augusta university is a conglomerate of many amazing things. The staff and professors truly admire their students and wish them the best. Its so beautiful to see them band together to make sure everyone gets what they need to succeed!

ai_summary: Aurora University offers a supportive and close-knit academic environment , particularly valued for its small class sizes and dedicated professors who actively engage with students. Many reviews highlight the strong community feel , where personal connections with peers and faculty thrive, enhancing the learning experience. The university is noted for its exceptional social work program and various resources, such as tutoring and academic advising, which aid student success. While the affordability of tuition is appreciated, some mention that the financial aid department can be challenging to navigate. Student life is characterized by numerous activities and clubs, though the campus atmosphere is described as quieter and sometimes lacking in entertainment options. Overall, Aurora University appeals to those seeking a personalized education and a strong sense of belonging.

reviews.text: I personally love the campus, and I really love how helpful the staff are. They don't make it seem like it's just a job; they really do care for the students and their well-being. I love how every month they have events; it keeps the campus life going. I love the library and the amount of resources they have
Aurora is a pretty solid place to go to school. It does take some effort to have and find fun and parties and in general that scene, but if you make friends with the right people it’s possible.

I love all the hands-on activities we do in classes as a Health Science (HLS) and Communication Sciences and Disorders (CSD) major. In anatomy we have models and Syndavers that go along with our coursework and in Audiology I've gotten to learn how to conduct audiometry tests and how to use an otoscope. As for changes, I would love to see more professors in the CSD field. Currently we only have 2 but I think it would be beneficial for students to learn from a variety of professionals in the field.
They were very helpful with communicating during the enrollment process. They are very easy to get ahold of if I have any questions too, which I have had a lot of questions!

The small class sizes make it really easy to get to know your professors and ask questions during and after classes.
Cost of living off-campus around here is very high, unfortunately.

Aurora University offers a variety of programs that can be taken online for people who want to fit academics into their busy lives. As a first generation older daughter who's running around with errands and working two full time jobs in order to make ends meet, Aurora University really gave me hope to dream again, and bring those dreams into reality. I am able to work on classes during my free time, and slowly progress towards my academic goals. The counselors and professor are understanding with my schedule and continuously going out their way to find resources in order to make sure I'm successful during every semester. There are a plethora of resources that are offered on campus that are also offered online, such as appointments with the writing center and math tutors via zoom. The financial aid and students accounts team is really on top of their communication and are constantly willing to help. Overall, it has been a memorable one year, and I'm confident i'll finish strong.
I have found the balance between school and and work as well as my personal life, i think AU is a great schoool offers a lot of inclusivity and unison among the students.
Overall, Aurora University is great for people who want to go to a smaller campus. It comes with more tight knit communities and great connections.
Truly have had a great experience at Aurora. Love the library, resources available to students, and the professor to student ratio in classrooms.

Overall, my experience so far at Aurora University has been positive, and I believe this campus offers a valuable educational environment. One of the most significant advantages of Aurora University is its commitment to small class sizes. This develops a more personalized learning experience, allowing for meaningful interactions with professors who are genuinely invested in their students' success. Furthermore, the university has a great diverse range of clubs and organizations that allows a great campus life, offering more opportunities for students to engage in activities and build lasting relationships.
While my experience so far has been favorable, there are areas where improvements could be made. Specifically, increasing the number of weekend activities and events would further enhance the overall student experience. The introduction of more concerts, guest speakers, or themed events could help build more relationships and make the campus life more enjoyable.
Prejudice University. If you’re black and not an athlete run to another university. Inexperienced counselors and definitely not military friendly

Aurora University Student Review
I’ve been attending Aurora University for three years now, and overall, it’s been a great experience. While the campus is small, there are plenty of activities and opportunities to get involved and meet new people—which has been really important for me as I’ve grown into adult life.
I work on campus, which has been both convenient and helpful. The job hours are short and flexible, making it easy to balance work with my class schedule.
One of the best parts of my time here has been getting involved in sports and clubs. That’s where I’ve made most of my friends, and I’m really thankful for those experiences. I’ve also been lucky to have two professors who have become mentors to me. They genuinely care and have supported me through challenges—something I’ve found to be true about most of the faculty here. The professors truly want the best for their students.
My experience at Aurora University has been good thus far. Everything I need is easily accessible. I like the fact that it's a small, close-knit community.

Since attending AU since fall of 23’, the experience has been nice especially the campus life and the scenery. Staying on campus it gives you not only the chance to take ahold of becoming that young adult and also making the correct decision for your future. The academic system is wonderful especially getting a young professor’s or even someone that takes their time and elaborates on the topic very well, or getting one that is interested in helping willing and want to give us student a chance of learning without popping our brains.

Aurora University was a great school for a non-traditional student such as myself looking to get my bachelors degree in a timely manner without breaking the bank. My advisors and professors were helpful in setting up my coursework and classes. I knew what the next two years of college life would like because they had it all planned out making it easy for life decisions within that two year mark. All of my professors were attentive and understanding. The coursework was neither easy nor hard but it did take time and commitment to keep up your GPA. The online forum was easy to use and had rare occurrences of needing IT support.
I can't add much about the student life but I can say that I did get a lot of student life emails about the performing arts and other surrounding activities, which I thought was a neat way for students to stay connected.
Truthfully as an online student, I wouldn't change anything when it comes to Aurora's online programs.

ai_summary: Azusa Pacific University (APU) has a strong sense of community , with many students commenting on the warm, welcoming atmosphere and the genuine care from both faculty and peers. The small class sizes foster close relationships, allowing for personal attention from professors who are often described as dedicated and supportive. APU's Christian foundation influences academics and campus life, with required chapel attendance being a notable aspect for many. While the tuition is considered high, many students feel that the educational quality and personal growth opportunities justify the cost. However, some reviews mention challenges such as a lack of diversity , issues with campus safety, and a disconnect for commuter students. Overall, APU is seen as a place where students can find support, build lasting friendships, and grow in their academic and spiritual lives.

reviews.text: The biology program will challenge students to become the best student that they can be. Student life was good, but I believe that once football returns to the school in Fall of 2026 that student life will be even better.

Azusa Pacific University has absolutely surpassed my expectations of what going back to school could look like. Both faculty and students are extremely considerate about others and genuinely care about student success and making the campus a better place. Although there are not as many class time selections available as desired, APU puts a lot of work into ensuring their classes are well-organized and beneficial to student growth. I love that Coug Walk is a place for different clubs and sections of campus can connect with new students and offer multiple events throughout the year, as well as the fact that students can help support each other in academic and personal development. Truly, this school provides a space for students to learn and grow, while keeping Christ at the center of education and leading students towards career paths where they can flourish in their skill.

The main draw of APU is its community. The students that go here are some of the best people I've ever met. In that sense, APU functions more like a college camp, since the dorming options and food options are adequate. I wouldn't prefer to go anywhere else though.

Very limited dining options, barely any student organizations/clubs, dead campus life, and little opportunities for students to get involved. I do not see “God first” reflected in their students or academics. Go to a public university if you can for a better education, and better everything! I feel like I was suffering everyday at APU, my time there was not worth the overpriced tuition.

I am currently still in my first year at Azusa Pacific University. I am enrolled as a nursing student in their Upper Division Transfer BSN Program, meaning I already have a bachelor's degree and am going back for a second one. So far, I have thoroughly enjoyed my time at the school. I think the student to staff ratio is perfect and most of the professors are wonderful.

I like how the staff is so kind and willing to help students whoever something I wish we can change is financial aid information because some of the staff promise to help with financial assistance but there are some misunderstandings in expenses that make it unclear what needs to be paid.
I truly have enjoyed my time here at APU. The people are so welcoming and all of my professors have known me by name which I think facilitates my learning in a safe environment.

My overall experience with Azusa Pacific has been a positive experience, as I have found an incredibly welcoming community that has made me feel at ease. However, I do wish that the university would provide some enrichment for the students in the form of events or facilities.
No social life. There is no student who values the ethics the school puts out. Mostly the class is quiet. Wasted my life and time here
The place is just so naturistic and majestic. It may not be as big as Citrus College, but this university is better in many ways.

While I've only been at APU for four weeks now, I've felt very welcomed. I'm already in a tight group of people on my floor and in my majors (I'm double majoring in Acting and Honors Humanities). APU does a great job of fostering a sense of kinship and community (you'll hear that work lots f you come here 😉), and I already feel as if I've learned a ton in my classes. Loving it so far, and I'm sure you will too!

I would say the only real struggles I've had from APU are due to major-specific professors. I just haven't felt very seen by them at all. However, that is only one part of APU, and most people will not run into them. I have absolutely loved all of my other professors. They truly care about their students and will go out of their way to help you. I also love the students here and the community environment. I think that is what makes APU stand out from other schools.
Great school just need a chance to be able to afford it it’s a bit pricey. School doesn’t have the ability to make smaller payments

The dining options on campus are very limited, overpriced, and lack quality. Security is minimal, with open access to campus making it feel less safe. In addition, much of the administration staff is unhelpful, leaving students frustrated. Overall, the school experience has been disappointing. For a university whose slogan is “God First”, many students feel it is more accurately “Money First”, as tuition is extremely high with little support for those in need of additional financial assistance. Given the cost, one would expect higher standards for safety, food, and living conditions, but these expectations are not met. Many students have also expressed concerns about the lack of inclusivity and tolerance for those who differ from the school’s expectations. For a university that emphasizes living a life that reflects the heart of Christ, it is disheartening to see students who do not fit a narrow standard being shunned or disapproved of.

At Azusa Pacific University, I had a fantastic freshman year. The best part about the school in my experience was the sense of community. Everyday at the start of my fall semester I met new people who were so welcoming, genuine, and fun to be around. The professors are there for you academically and spiritually which is hard to find at any other college. I highly recommend APU to anyone who is looking find a family of people who care for you on a deeper level. Just after being there for 8 months, I have made friends who will last me a lifetime.

ai_summary: Baker University stands out for its strong sense of community and personalized education . Students often highlight the small class sizes and supportive professors who are invested in their success, creating opportunities for meaningful connections. The campus itself is described as beautiful and fostering a cozy atmosphere, although some wish for more activities and diversity both in the student body and food options. Academically, programs like nursing and exercise science receive praise for their quality and hands-on learning opportunities . Student life is active, with plenty of ways to get involved, particularly through Greek life and athletic programs, but some note that the town's limited offerings can lead to boredom. While many enjoy their experiences, a few raise concerns about administration and campus resources, including outdated facilities and food quality. Overall, Baker University is recognized as a place where students can thrive academically while feeling like part of a close-knit family .

reviews.text:
I attended Baker University as a psychology major from 2019-2023.
The psychology department itself had some genuinely caring professors who knew their material. The campus in Baldwin City is beautiful—historic buildings, tree-lined paths, that small liberal arts college feel. Baker is expensive. I was working 25-30 hours a week on top of a full course load. I missed study groups and campus activities because I was working. The "college experience" everyone talks about? I didn't get that.
When COVID hit, everything fell apart. We were sent home mid-semester, classes went online, but tuition stayed the same. I was paying full price for a Zoom education. The university offered minimal financial flexibility despite students losing campus jobs and family incomes cratering.
If you come from money or have significant scholarships, Baker might work for you. The education itself is decent. But if you're a student who needs to work your way through college, seriously reconsider.

My experience has been great, I've met my lifelong friends there. There has been a few up and downs, but everything won't be perfect. There could be a change in the cafeteria food, but overall it has been great.

Baker is a very beautiful school but has a lot of problems they try to cover up. To start when touring campus they try to make it seem very lively and fun. I've been going here for about 3 months and everything I have been told has so far not been true. I understand they have to do this to draw people in but I didn't think it would be this bad. I think my biggest problem with baker is that their financial aid and business office has no idea what they are doing. It took the business office about 2 months for them to figure out what I owed, I went in person to see them multiple times and they had me on the wrong meal plan, housing and other things. The financial aid office has been absolutely horrible they are always annoyed when you come in and act like you are an inconvenience. I have had multiple scholarships sent in and had to be resent because they were lost, the week or spring registration they put holds on my account for documents I signed in July they lost, and the list goes on.

Overall, the campus and city are beautiful. Its small, so its definitely a more intimate environment. Depending on what your preferences are, that small town vibe can definitely be nice. You get lots of opportunities to get involved with the community and build relationships with the people around you.

As I am an incoming freshman, I haven’t technically seen anything as of yet. Going on my visit though, the campus is beautiful and they have many different kinds of dorms which are all nice. The only thing I would like to see change is adding more for the town of Baldwin, there’s not much to do there.

I liked the faculty; each Teacher cared about you especially if you took the class serious. They held you accountable but guided you and challenged you effectively. The campus was always beautiful, the church was nice to connect with others, the activities were inclusive and it's a small place so it not overwhelming.

Computer systems and integration with moodle and other course related systems for reading books, live plan, etc. can be improved. Lots of jumping around which is frustrating even once used to it. Since they lost all their computers systems they're unable to provide students options on degree completion programs, and students who want to leave Baker are held hostage because they cannot produce transcripts.
Baker University in my experience is a very cozy and small college. The professors are all very kind and understanding.

Life on campus at Baker University is vibrant and welcoming, offering students a close-knit community where academics, friendships, and personal growth thrive. The small class sizes create strong relationships between students and professors, ensuring personalized learning experiences. Campus life is active with student organizations, athletic events, and traditions like the annual "Parmenter Hall Lighting." Baker’s beautiful, historic campus in Baldwin City provides a peaceful setting while still offering plenty of activities. Whether studying in the library, grabbing coffee with friends, or participating in community service, students find countless ways to connect, get involved, and create lasting memories at Baker University.

The professors are amazing and really love their jobs. The only complaints I have are technological difficulties that reoccur but I have been told this doesn’t happen often so this is most likely a one time occurrence.

My review with Baker university has not been all that grand. The portal has been down since December which means students have not been able to access anything for their account and it has been super inconvenient. It seems like this is not a top priority for whoever is running the situation because it's still down. This also leads to issues in my personal classes and I am having to do more than necessary just to complete an assignment. This is honestly a joke and I wish I did not choose this school. Apparently the portal is down because there was some type of cyber security breach and that is all Baker is telling us. Someone even started a petition about it because it is just out of hand. This school sucks. Don't go here. You probably can't even apply and do paperwork right now if you wanted to. Hopefully we get some type of reimbursement because of all of this.

I would say that my experience at Baker University has been wonderful, as a somewhat shy person being on the soccer team here and joining one of the fraternities has really pushed me out of my shell. The campus is wonderful and lots of amazing teachers.

From my experience, it's a lovely campus filled with positive, encouraging people and a supportive environment. Academically, it is a solid school with several excellent professors. However, the school leans more toward a traditional, old-school approach to education.
Many professors follow conventional methods; for instance, only a few use slides or offer Zoom classes. If a student misses a class due to athletic commitments, they must rely on their classmates for notes or turn to the textbook, as recordings or supplemental materials are not typically provided. Additionally, grades are not consistently updated throughout the term—they are only made available during midterms and finals week.
That said, the school excels in many ways. It offers great professors, small class sizes, and a conducive learning environment that fosters academic growth. Overall, it’s a fantastic institution for those who value a traditional, community-oriented education experience.

Baker is such an amazing campus that feels like home. There are so many opportunities that the campus provides and you will leave Baker and become a much better individual throughout your journey at Baker.

Baker has a very friendly and welcoming environment. It’s very easy to connect with people in the community as well as those on campus. It’s easy to get involved and meet new people. The town is small but it’s very nice. Sometimes I wish there was more stuff to do in town, but if you have a good group of people it’s still a good time.

ai_summary: Ball State University offers a vibrant campus life with a strong sense of community , making it easy for students to meet new people and get involved in activities. Reviews consistently highlight the supportive and approachable faculty who genuinely care about students' success, particularly in popular programs like architecture, business, and education. While the campus is recognized for its modern facilities and beautiful scenery , some students expressed frustrations with the parking situation and dining options. The university is lauded for its affordability compared to other institutions , making it an attractive choice for many students. However, there are calls for improvements in diversity and better responsiveness to certain student concerns. Overall, students appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and the resources available for both academic and personal development.

reviews.text: Ball state university is an amazing college! There is plenty of acidic majors to choose from with some of the best professors who show that they care about the students. The campus is great with plenty of options for entertainment and food choices.

When I went on a tour it was really fun! I learned a lot, got connected with a lot of people who had the same interests in me. The students that did the tour was extremely nice as well. 10/10 experience.

My experience at Ball State honestly taught me a lot about myself. It was my first real taste of independence, and even though it wasn’t always easy, I liked that it pushed me to grow up and figure things out on my own. Being on campus made me realize what I’m capable of and what I actually want for my future. I liked being around other students who were all working toward something. It made me feel motivated. Some of my classes were challenging, but I actually appreciated that because it helped me reflect on what I’m really interested in. Being there made me realize I want a career that’s meaningful and where I can actually help people. If I could change something, I would say I wish there was more clear guidance about finances. Tuition, loans, and figuring out how to pay for everything can feel overwhelming, especially when you’re doing a lot of it on your own. More support or clearer communication about financial planning would really help students stress less and focus more.
I have had more fun at Ball State than I have in my whole life. There are so many different groups of people here, and I found my friends very quickly! 10/10 recommend!
I enjoy how Ball State University has a diverse campus with diverse opportunities. Ball State has many resources available to everyone.
It is a pretty good campus with a good community but it lacks a lot of things to do outside of the campus.

My experience at Ball State University has been fantastic. The nursing program is well-structured and combines strong academic learning with real-world clinical experience. The professors are knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in students’ success, which made learning both challenging and rewarding. One of the highlights was the hands-on clinical rotations, where I gained valuable experience caring for patients in a variety of healthcare settings. These experiences not only strengthened my nursing skills but also taught me the importance of empathy, communication, and teamwork.

Ball State has strong programs, especially in education, and many professors care about their students. However, disabled or chronically ill students face real challenges. Accommodations exist but often require constant self-advocacy. Transportation, parking, and campus accessibility frequently don’t meet students’ needs. Housing is another concern: dorms aren’t consistently accessible, accommodations are hard to get, and noise or room placement can worsen health issues. Administrative communication is often slow, leaving students to manage barriers on their own. Ball State can work for self-advocates, but students with complex medical or housing needs should expect extra obstacles.
I've had a great experience as an Ed major here at Ball State. Everyone is friendly and there's so many opportunities to socialize.
I'm a freshman right now, but I can say that I like it here. The professors are engaging and I love my classes
At Ball State, there are many classes you can take to better your education based on your major, Ball State gives you opportunities to give you a successful job and career.

Ball state has been a good school. The professors seem to actaully care, and want to see you graduate. There are a lot of resources for students for everything. I also like how nig the campus is. The campus is not big or small, it is a perfect size to walk or take the bus to your classes. I would like to see more diversity in the food at Ball State.
Ball State was the best pick for me due to the smaller campus. The students and staff there are very lively and I love to see people when its warm outside and people out and picnic.

Ball State has been everything I hoped it would be and more. I'm struggling a little bit to afford the rest of my education but I'm hoping to perservere and chug along! It's a fantastic campus and 9/10 professors are really great! The campus is very walkable and the busses are mostly great. Also the food is very good! I'd love to see some changes to the handling of our DEI programs and such. The administration is pretty meh, I wish they'd take solid stances on very important world issues. Hugh Jackman is a pretty big fan of the campus now though and that's pretty cool!

My experience at Ball State was rich and quite varied. I had the opportunity to take classes in the Theatre Department as well as courses in the Honors College. The research and skills I learned in these classes was very valuable. I especially liked how many of my classes taught me to think critically and I use this skill in every aspect of my life. In the future, I would like to see more attention paid to student clubs. I felt that there was a high bar of entry for many extracurricular clubs at this school.

ai_summary: At Barry University, students appreciate the intimate and supportive learning environment fostered by small class sizes, which allows for meaningful interactions with professors . There's a strong emphasis on diversity and inclusivity , providing a rich cultural experience for students from various backgrounds. While academics and dedicated faculty receive high praise, some students voice concerns about the organization of administration and financial aid, highlighting difficulties with communication and support in these areas. Campus life offers a blend of events and activities, although some feel the student life could use more vibrancy , and the food options may need improvement. Overall, the university is seen as a welcoming place that sets the stage for personal and academic growth, despite some areas needing enhancement.

reviews.text: I like everything I've seen so far because this is a new experience for me like my theater friends, the dorms are comfy and I like the roommate agreements, I also feel safe and my family doesn't have to worry about me. What I would change is the food because sometimes is not good and how expensive the tuition and housing is, especially for International students who don't have the same opportunities as a citizen and can't apply for grants or working on-campus because we're not citizens.

From the moment that I met them, I knew that I wanted to attend Barry. The recruiters were excellent. Once I started class, it got better. My adviser ensured that I was in all my classes and that I was adjusting well. The student body is a mixture of cultures. Even as an online student, you are still very much a part of the body, and that is what makes Barry an excellent choice.
Barry University is a great school overall, but i did not have much success with financial aid for the athletic department. But I did have a wonderful experience for the most part.

When I first arrived at Barry University I kept an open mind. It offers tons of opportunities to get involved on campus. I recently joined C.A.B a club dedicated to make sure students are being heard when it comes to activities. One thing I loved was the different food options. Another thing I noticed was how student life is very inactive. Hopefully more students will get involved and make the experience better.

It's one if the best academic institutions you can ever be admitted into.it offers several courses from educational,medical sciences,general legal studies and many others.Its surrounding is therapeutic and conducive environment to study.I urge all international students to come forward and apply to join this prestigious institution.You can all be granted a fully funded 4 year course programme including accommodation where you'll incur no costs.Looking forward to meeting you there!!

I am a graduate student at Barry University in an MFA program, and I am very satisfied with the program's quality. I also appreciate the flexibility that professors offer adult students who need to balance their studies with work, family, and production for their classes.

I have been at Barry university since August 2023 and it’s a perfect quiet and laid back private school if you want to be away from the party scene. In the classes it’s about less than 30 students in each class and gives more opportunity for one on one with professors.
I completed an MBA and a PhD here. All I can say is that my experience was excellent in every aspect.

My experience here at Barry has been lovely. I've gotten so much support with personal injuries and class. I've also met a lot of new people and created a support system here when I had no one. It's been a pleasure here, and with this scholarship, I could continue that and further pursue my academics.

My overall experience at Barry University has been nothing short of amazing. One of the things I appreciate most is the vibrant student life, where there is always a sense of community and support. From campus events to student organizations, there are countless opportunities to get involved and connect with others, which has made my time here both fulfilling and exciting.
I had always dreamed of attending an HBCU, but financially that wasn’t a realistic option for me. Coming to Barry, I was hoping to still find that same feeling of belonging and cultural appreciation. The diversity here is truly a 10/10, and it has exceeded my expectations. Being surrounded by students from so many different backgrounds makes me feel at home and reminds me that I made the right choice. Barry has given me not only a great education but also a campus environment that celebrates who I am and encourages me to grow alongside others

My overall experience at Barry University has been incredibly positive. As a pre-nursing major, I’ve received strong support throughout my academic journey—my professors, advisor, and the faculty have all played a huge role in helping me stay on track with my goals. It truly feels like everyone here wants to see me succeed, and for that, I’m deeply grateful. Even on tough days, the staff uplifted me and made me feel at home, which made a big difference in my college experience. I also love the diversity on campus, which has allowed me to connect with international students from all over the world; however, I would like to see more student engagement and campus activities, especially since our school is relatively small and doesn’t have many events.
My overall experience here at Barry has been good. Met many people from all over. Learned was to improve my academic performances and break out of my shy shell.

Excellent education, no one is left behind. The nursing program is very competitive and the professors are very knowledgeable and accommodating to the needs of the students. The campus is clean and welcoming to all. The library and the chapel is open to visitors and the campus is clean , there is plenty of parking and security that is available to students
great school easy process to enroll, they have pliantly of flexibility with there schedules and rapid start dates to all the programs that they offer.

I really love the bright and jovial environment of Barry University. I love the student life, the classrooms. When you arrive you are greeted with nothing but love and kindness. You find your clique on the first day because they are all so welcoming. The professors are the sweet people ever. What I would like to see change would possibly be the food. That’s pretty much eveything is else is a 10 out of 10

ai_summary: Baylor University is often described as a welcoming and close-knit community, giving students a strong sense of belonging and home. Many reviews highlight the dedication and approachability of professors , emphasizing their personal attention and genuine interest in student success across various disciplines. Academically, students find Baylor to be challenging but ultimately rewarding, with a supportive environment that includes numerous resources to aid in learning. The campus is frequently praised for its beauty and the vibrant student life , which includes a variety of extracurricular activities and strong school spirit. However, some reviewers note that the tuition can be steep and express concerns about the lack of diversity within the student body. Additionally, while many students appreciate the Christian atmosphere, others feel that the religious aspects can sometimes be a deterrent for those less inclined toward such beliefs. Overall, Baylor University is viewed as an exceptional place for personal and academic growth, but may not suit everyone’s preferences or expectations.

reviews.text: I visited Baylor university and it was a wonderful experience. I visited around 7pm and I loved it. The campus was very nice and a lot of students there were gathering around different places and having lots of fun. People were welcoming and willing to socialize with others. All around everyone was having a good time. Everywhere I went had students socializing so if you are social Baylor, is a great place.
My overall experience hasn’t been great. However, that doesn’t mean it is a bad university. Everyone has a different experience, and it just happened to not favor me!

I loved my time at Baylor! It has the. benefits of a large school with a distinctly Christian focus. There are so many fun traditions that enhance the student experience like Homecoming, Sing, and Baylor Athletics!
I love Baylor for so many reasons. I love that it is a Christian community and I have loved being on this beautiful campus.
It was a great experience where I had exposure to many different types of educators and peers. I learned immensely in an environment that often challenged me.

I loved my time at Baylor and I did not fully understand how amazing it was until I graduated. Admittedly, I spent my last year or so feeling very tired of Baylor and being ready to graduate, but upon graduation and moving on to medical school, I realized how amazing and unique of a place Baylor was. I am Christian and that drew me to Baylor, which is something I embraced at the school and was thankful for. I think when I was there I grew disillusioned with the commercialization and what I perceived as a high level on conformity amongst the Baylor student population in regards to their faith, which made me become more cynical about it than I should have. However, once I got out into the real world, I realized what an incredible and unique space Baylor creates to live and learn in a Christian community, even with some of the issues I had with it. I cannot recommend the school enough to anyone seeking an Christian collegiate experience.

BU is an excellent university. What I like the most is the Geosciences faculty—they are very professional, highly knowledgeable, and provide a strong academic environment. The overall staff at Baylor is also excellent and supportive. In addition, I love the campus; it is beautiful and well maintained. My favorite building is the Baylor Sciences Building (BSB), where I spend most of my time as a graduate student. Overall, Baylor has been a great place for my academic growth and experience.

My overall experience has been good.Campus is beautiful and professors are helpful. I would like to see change in the dinning halls such as better food/snack options. Also, tuition is is on the higher side.

Baylor University is a great college and helps students grow as a person. Not only does Baylor grow you as a person but it grows you as a human being and prepares you for the rest of your life after graduation.

I think Baylor University is a wonderful campus with a great community. Most of the professors are very kind and understanding. However, as a vegetarian, the dining options are very limited and not very nutritious.

My time at Baylor University was overall a great experience. I studied integrative biology on the pre-medicine track. The classes were all well-structured and designed, and taught by professors who were experts in their respective fields. The overarching degree plan included many medical school prerequisites and prepared you well for the rigor of many graduate programs. I attended from 2018 to 2022, so my time there was unfortunately affected by the COVID-19 epidemic, but Baylor responded to it well. We transitioned to online courses for about a year, which I would say did take away from the overall learning and student experience. With that said, I would not put any blame on the university for that, for they did the best they could in my opinion. The city where Baylor is located has its ups and downs, but I found it to be a good fit. The athletics department was phenomenal during my time attending. I would highly recommend Baylor to anyone considering going.
I loved the sense of community I received there. Everyone was very friendly and open to lending a helping hand.
Lots of finicial burden. But a good Christian college. But if u have the money u have to apply to their business school. It is really nice.
Overall great campus and really nice professors. I suggest if you’re a freshman you live in collins. Dinings halls are really good also.

My experience at Baylor so far has been both exciting and challenging in the best way. Transferring in as a Communications major, I immediately felt how strong Baylor’s sense of community is. Professors genuinely care about students, and classes push me to think critically, ask better questions, and grow as a storyteller. Being involved in student media has been one of the most meaningful parts of my time here. Contributing stories and working as a news anchor has helped me gain real-world experience while still feeling supported as I learn. Baylor has also helped me grow personally. Moving to Waco pushed me outside my comfort zone, but it taught me independence, confidence, and resilience. The campus culture encourages faith, leadership, and service, which has shaped how I see my future and my role in journalism. Overall, Baylor has given me opportunities, mentorship, and a strong foundation for my career.

ai_summary: Belhaven University offers a close-knit , Christian environment where students appreciate the personal attention from professors and smaller class sizes. Many reviews highlight the supportive community , where faculty are described as genuinely invested in students' success and well-being. Students often mention the strong ties they form with peers and faculty alike, creating a family-like atmosphere . However, concerns about the affordability of tuition and some administrative challenges, particularly in student services, surfaced in several reviews. The campus is noted for its beautiful surroundings , though some desire improvements in dining options. Overall, students value the unique blend of faith , academics, and community that Belhaven provides.

reviews.text: Professors are great, campus events are usually fun, and the food is decent enough, but it does not make up for the housing experience. They'll charge you between 4 and 6k for the semester (standard for colleges), but they don't include ANY of the amenities you'd expect to see at that price. Beds are uncomfortable and super cheap, only one working water fountain, there was a rat living in one of the buildings for an entire semester, if a washer or dryer breaks, no matter how much you report it to maintenance, they will take months to even look at it. The main issue is maintenance. If you report something to maintenance, they will give it the ol' "college try," not resolve the issue, and close the ticket. If the one person they send to look at the issue doesn't immediately find the issue upon walking into the room, they'll deem you wrong and REFUSE to take another look at the issue.

Belhaven is an amazing school. Since the very beginning of my admissions process I've felt supported. The faculty makes sure they provide you with all the information you need to know or direct you to the correct person or place. The upperclassmen are especially welcoming with helping new students get around campus okay. No matter where you turn you are bound to see a friendly face. If I am able to I will obtain my master's degree at Belhaven as well. They offer a 5th year full tuition scholarship as well! They operate with love and intention. I couldn't have made a better choice.

I loved my experience with Belhaven. The campus atmostphere is so positive and uplifting, and it is easy to find a community here. All of the professors care deeply about students' educations and will do everything they can to make sure a student is successful.

Belhaven University is one of the flagship schools of christendom in the U.S. Committed Belhaven students are involved in many clubs and organizations that aim to encourage the search for Truth and the furtherance of God's kingdom on the individual and global scale. Staff are ready and willing to serve and seek to provide students with all the amenities they deserve. If you are looking for a warm welcome, effectual encouragement, and spiritual growth, put Belhaven at the top of your list!

I absolutely love this school! The schools security is so amazing! All of the professors that I have had here have been so wonderful when it comes to caring for their students and having a genuine will to serve the students in whatever way they can. I have learned so much about God here. There are not many clubs but the ones we have are great! The food could be better but overall not bad. I have furthered my relationship so much with God here and I am so grateful for the opportunities I have been given.

My overall experience at Belhaven University was transformative. The Christian Ministry in Social Services program offered a unique blend of academic depth, spiritual formation, and hands-on preparation for service-oriented leadership. The faculty were accessible, encouraging, and truly committed to helping students excel. Completing the program and graduating Cum Laude strengthened my sense of calling and equipped me with practical tools for community work and ministry. While more built-in fieldwork opportunities would have enhanced the program, the overall environment was supportive, challenging, and deeply grounding for my personal, academic, and spiritual growth
I do my schooling all online right now and I am very pleased with the overall flexibility, directness, and ease of the programs available.
The campus was beautiful, the people were nice, the professors were friendly. Overall, a wonderful, beautiful time

I have enjoyed Belhaven so far. I just wish there was less of a hesistance to celebrate diversity and different ways to worship God, and I wish the songs we sing at chapel were more fun. I also don't know how I feel about the college going a bit too hard with their reliance on Christianity, not at all acknowledging that many students at Belhaven don't believe in the Christian God. I think there should be alternatives.

My overall experience at Belhaven University has been positive. The small class sizes and supportive professors create a welcoming environment where students feel encouraged to succeed. I appreciate the strong Christian foundation, which is integrated into academics and campus life, helping me grow both academically and spiritually. While there can sometimes be challenges with administrative processes, the sense of community and the personal attention from faculty and staff make Belhaven a place where students can truly thrive.

I’ve had a great experience at Belhaven! Coming from out of state, it’s been a home away from home and a place where I’ve met many close friends. The campus is super tight knit and welcoming from day one! People took the time to remember my name and what my major was.
There are so many activities to do outside of classes and on the weekend! Personally, I’ve enjoyed getting involved in Reformed University Fellowship (RUF), one of the campus ministries at Belhaven! RUF has weekly large group meetings on Tuesdays with a time of worship, a short message, and a time of fellowship!
If you’re looking for a place to grow in your relationships with God, with others, and in your academics, Belhaven is the perfect place for you!
I don’t know how belhaven University is because I haven’t started yet. I’m a transfer student, but I did visit the campus and I went to orientation. I truly love the campus and the students.

Belhaven University presents itself as a Christian institution, but my experience was filled with frustration, hypocrisy, and a complete lack of support. They were nearly impossible to reach by phone or email. I had a debt from nearly a decade ago that I included in a legal bankruptcy, yet Belhaven somehow transferred the small 3 percent balance owed back from collections to the school and used it to block my transcript, preventing me from moving forward with my education. Despite tuition being close to $30,000 per year, the education I received at Belhaven was the least sufficient and least organized of the five universities I’ve attended. Their administrative process is disorganized and lacks transparency. For a school that claims to represent Christian values, they showed no grace, empathy, or understanding. I would not recommend this school to anyone. There are far better, more affordable, and more ethical institutions out there that will treat you like a human being.

Belhaven used to be a great Christian school. Those days are gone. Tuition is too high. Over 90% of the staff is mean, rude, and nasty especially Financial Aid. FYI, YOU DO NOT OWN OUR MONEY, PEASANT WORKERS.
All of the good employees are no longer their. DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME APPLYING TO THAT DISGUSTING SCHOOL. JESUS DOES NOT LIVE IN THOSE SNAKES HEARTS. THERE ARE MORE ACCEPTING, WARM, AFFORDABLE, AND KIND UNIVERSITIES OUT THERE.

It was really nice to tour, but I just found out I cannot afford all of it and they were the only school to accept me. I really do need help, my family doesn't make much money and I've had multiple jobs myself.

ai_summary: At Bellarmine University, students frequently highlight the strong sense of community and support from both peers and faculty. The small class sizes foster personal relationships with professors, who are described as caring and approachable, making it easier for students to seek help and guidance. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and inviting atmosphere , with plenty of opportunities for involvement through clubs and activities. However, recurring concerns include high tuition costs , a need for improved food options, and a desire for greater campus diversity . Some students also express frustrations regarding the handling of student feedback and the transition to more online learning during the pandemic. Overall, Bellarmine's emphasis on academic success, personal growth, and a welcoming community sets it apart in the eyes of current and former students.

reviews.text: I picked Bellarmine University for its huge scholarships and there is not a day that goes by that I don't regret I went anywhere else. Without factoring in affordability (that's in case you get a scholarship) Bellarmine does not hold a candle to other institutions. Options for dining are sparse, and for the little variety you get, the food simply is not good. Staff are extremely friendly though; they are truly what makes BU not that bad. The campus is beautiful from the outside, but from an inside perspective, it's depressing. Buildings outside of Centro have terrible upkeep. Winter maintenance is nonexistent. I remember walking into my Kennedy dorm for the first time and bursting into tears because I was sharing an old TINY-you can touch the other bed from your own- dorm with two other girls. The WOW program is torture. You are forced to keep up with a portfolio as well as unnecessary, annoying, and repetitive "Knight Life" classes. The student body is largely rich and white.

My experience at Bellarmine has more impactful and fulfilling that I ever could've imagined. As soon as you step foot on the campus, there is overwhelming support system from everyone there. All of the faculty, staff, and students want each other to succeed and will provide assistance in helping you do so through various different ways. They prioritize students mental health and overall well-being along with providing the necessary resources to help students be successful in the classroom as well. The smaller campus and class sizes allow students to build strong connections with their peers and professors that aid in their success along with making Bellarmine feel more like home.

Bellarmine is a great school for academics of course, they have a great community, very welcoming here & really try to make you feel included. Professors are very helpful & know their stuff. I’d recommend the school.
My experience with Bellarmine has been amazing. The people here are so nice and kind. The professor here are always there for me when I need them.

My overall experience here was amazing! I transferred here my junior year from a different university. I was obviously nervous coming to a different school because I was not starting out as a freshman and I thought it would make it harder to make friends. My thoughts were deemed false because Bellarmine made it easy for me to make connections. With the smaller class sizes and the attention the teachers give to the students, I felt comfortable in my first classes here. The clubs and organizations I joined also gave me a chance to meet other students in my grade and make friends. I am so thankful for coming to Bellarmine because everyone here has helped me to achieve my goals and graduate with my BA in Psychology.

My experience so far at Bellarmine has been great. I have had many opportunities to engage in different programs such as the Pioneer Scholars Program, Week of Welcome (WOW), Peer Coach/Mentor, the Ambassador Program, and the Accounting Association. One of the most valuable opportunities I have had was being part of the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program this spring, where I was able to give back to the community by providing services to low-income individuals. I am truly grateful to be part of VITA because, while many of the activities I am involved in on campus are valuable, they mainly impact the Bellarmine community. VITA has allowed me to expand my abilities and help improve the broader community. What I would hope to change is to see more students majoring in accounting. Often, students view accounting as extremely difficult, and I hope there is a way to make the field of study less intimidating.
I love Bellarmine and couldn’t feel more at home there. I wish we had more food options on campus. There’s always something to do no matter the day or time.

I love Bellarmine! BU Mae’s sure you feel included no matter what is going on on campus, whether it’s hanging out in centro and giving you someone to sit with or needing help with work in the tutoring center they are always so welcoming and helpful!

I love Bellarmine because I have found a life and community here. I was able to make great friends, have a strong relationship with my professors, RSOs, and other academic leaders. But I will say the dorm experience has not been my favorite.

Bellarmine has been great for me. The city is close enough that there are events and activities you can go to, but far enough that you don't deal with any problems that occur in the city. The campus is walkable and has all sorts of resources for students to use.

MY overall experience has been really good, all the professors and staff are nice and welcoming.And there always some sort of activity happening in campus, which is perfect for a commuter like me that has long hour gaps between classes.

I absolutely love attending Bellarmine! The staff and faculty are wonderful and actually care about student success. My teachers know me by name and want to see me grow. There is a LOT to get involved in on campus and any student I have met thus far have been delightful.

I like the college, I just wish we had more resources since we are a private school. I wish the food was better and cater to Muslims. I wish our studies would focus on more world topics that are going on. But overall the campus is great easy to walk to places, and we are close to Bardstown road.
I love my experience at Bellarmine. The people are amazing; the community and belonging is there. I love working with everyone especially as a as an RA being an RSO leader.

Bellarmine provides a high quality education with an active athletics teams but very little social offerings. Greek life is essentially nonexistent and all parties on-campus are sports team related. That being said, the professors are all incredible and very vested in their students futures, as small class sizes ensure that you create personal and one-on-one relationships. The food is above average and has dietary options for vegetarians and those with gluten-sensitivity. Most students live on campus, in relatively nice dorms, and the campus is very safe with public safety constantly patrolling and offering free rides. However, the price of tuition has increased twice within the last four years and therefore attending the university is only feasible for most people if significant scholarships are awarded.

ai_summary: Belmont University stands out for its strong sense of community and supportive faculty , with many students highlighting the caring relationships fostered by professors who are often active in their fields. The campus is described as beautiful and welcoming, located near the vibrant Nashville music scene , which provides ample networking opportunities, especially for those studying music and the arts. While the university has excellent programs in music and nursing , some students express concerns about class availability and the high cost of attendance, suggesting a need for more diverse course offerings and financial support. Dining options receive mixed reviews, with some noting that the food can be inconsistent. The school's Christian foundation influences campus culture, which some appreciate while others find restrictive. Overall, students enjoy their experiences largely due to the unique opportunities for personal and professional growth that Belmont offers.

reviews.text: I have truly enjoyed my time at Belmont. I transfered for my major and have loved my courses and all the research oppurtunities that I have had.

There are great opportunities that come with being a Belmont student. While the school loves to recognize this they don't like to give students enough time to utilize these opportunities (especially school of music) time management is very important.

Overall I have loved my time at Belmont. I have made incredible connections and relationships as well as had so many wonderful leadership opportunities. Academically I have both been challenged and felt like certain classes were a waste of my time, but I have been instructed by some great professors who are not just invested in my academic success but in my personal success as well. Within the business college I wish for a wider range of electives. Classes such as career and professional development did not feel like a good use of my time and education, and I would have preferred the opportunity to take a higher level class in economics, for example, that would have, in my opinion, better developed my learning portfolio. I believe career and professional development can be restructured to be more useful, as many things I spent time learning in that class I already knew and was proficient at.

I am only a freshman, but in my short time here, I have hit the ground running with new experiences provided to me by my professors and peers. All the professors in Curb College are either still practicing or recently retired, making conversations and lectures about the industry up to date. Also, so many ways to get involved on campus. A cappella groups, Greek Life, and clubs relating to careers or interests are so easy to join!

I had an incredible, transformative time at Belmont! It definitely helped me grow into my early adult years and into my professional career. I really started to find my place while at Belmont and found the community there to be impactful and loving. I had the time of my life!!
My experience in the Exercise Science department at Belmont University has been amazing! The professors are amazing and truly care about their students!

The Belmont experience has been great!
I have made some long lasting friendships here, and because of the class sizes I have been able to become close with professors! I can seek out questions, advice both inside and outside of school. It has been amazing knowing I can approach my professors and ask for help!
Especially is the math department (I’m a math major) that has been very helpful and supportive towards my goals as a student.

My experience at Belmont has been very positive. I think it’s important to come into college with a set goat it may change when you get to college but having one beforehand helped me realize how many people are there to help support you reach those goals. I think the closeness of this community is unlike any other college everyone knows each other and everyone is friendly. I think the administration could handle some of the more apparent issues with a little more speed. They will get to it eventually but it takes some time.

I think I’ve really found my place at Belmont. The professors really care about you and I have good relationships with my professors. Food hasn’t been the best but they’ve made a lot of changes this year. It’s cool being surrounded by people who love music and art and are so so talented. It’s definitely not for everyone but I can say that I’ve had a good time. I’ve learned a lot about the kind of person I want to be outside of academia which has been cool. I’ve definitely grown a lot at Belmont and made some the best friends I’ve ever made. It takes time to adjust to college of course but Belmont makes it easy if you’re open minded. They put on really cool events. It’s also cool being so close to the city because there’s lots to do off campus. I would definitely encourage people to come to Belmont. The size is perfect because it’s not overwhelming but you still meet new people all the time. Housing is pretty good..

Sometimes it’s hard to find community— for me it came from my on-campus job at the fitness and recreation center. You just really have to put yourself out there and actively seek out friends and your people will find you, I promise.
Best decision I've ever made. I have lived and learned to live everyday to the fullest and it has been nothing but an absolute dream.

I honestly love a Belmont! It has everything someone may need to be successful. It has manageable class sizes and tons of opportunities for students because of the area in Nashville it’s in. Sometimes the food may not be great, but there is so many options one can choose from. It’s definitely not the typical college life but I think it’s better!!

I loved campus life and I love the location of Belmont as well! I am a soon to be nursing alumni so cannot speak to other majors, but the nursing professors and curriculum is top notch. I feel very prepared and even at an advantage going into the job market currently!

I'm very glad I decided to attend Belmont University. I struggled to find a college that truly called out to me; however, now that I'm here, I can't imagine being anywhere else. The global persecution I've grown to know, the business' I love to to frequent, and the friends I can't wait to see are only a few of the things that make me positive that I made the right decision when I chose Belmont University. Go Bruins, and Spirit of Gold too!!!

One of the best things about Belmont is the faculty. I never felt like just a number in a class. My professors were active professionals in their fields, yet they always had time for coffee or a check-in. They don’t just teach; they mentor. They genuinely want to see you succeed and often go out of their way to connect you with their own networks. I was able to land three different internships during my time as an undergrad solely because of my professors' connections. That hands-on experience was the direct bridge that helped me secure my first job in my field immediately after graduation.

ai_summary: Bethel University in Minnesota is often described as having a welcoming and close-knit community , where small class sizes allow for meaningful interactions between students and professors. Many reviews highlight the supportive atmosphere and the faculty's dedication to student success, particularly in academic fields like nursing and business, though there are concerns regarding recent budget cuts that have led to fewer class options. While students appreciate the beautiful campus and the integration of faith into academics, some express discomfort with the lack of diversity and a perceived "Bethel bubble" culture that can feel isolating. Student life is characterized by limited nightlife options , leading to an exodus over weekends, but the presence of various campus organizations and athletic events helps to foster engagement. Overall, Bethel University is viewed as a great fit for those desiring a Christian-focused education, but it might not resonate with students seeking a more diverse or independent college experience.

reviews.text:
My overall experience at Bethel University – Minnesota has been very positive and formative both academically and personally. I appreciated the supportive learning environment, small class sizes, and faculty who were approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in student success. The integration of faith, ethics, and professional practice helped me grow not only in my field of study but also in character and leadership. I also valued the sense of community and the emphasis on service and accountability.
One area I would like to see improved is increased flexibility for working adults and graduate or professional students, such as more evening, hybrid, or online course options. Expanding access to practical, real-world training opportunities and streamlining administrative processes would also enhance the overall student experience. Overall, Bethel provided a strong foundation for my professional and personal growth.
I haven't been a student there yet, but all my siblings have or are, and it seems to be a great place.

I am a current PSEO student at Bethel University and all I can say is that I love Bethel! The community doesn't feel like a stereotypical college—it's better than that. It feels like a tight-knit community, where a majority of students connect through their love of Jesus Christ. A faith filled community with amazing people, faculty, and facilities.
I’ve been here for two years, every person is very nice and super helpful. I feel like they really care about me like when I confused, they come to help me for no any reason.

Bethel is a great school. The professors are outstanding, engaging, and truly care about the growth of the students. Your experience as Bethel is what you make it, if you put yourself out there, and find the right people your experience will abound with growth. It is an excellent place to transition into adulthood.
Bethel University is a great spot for any student looking to grow in their academics, faith, and social life. Bethel is known for building a great sense of community and academic excellence.

I have enjoyed being a part of Bethel. It is closer to my home, which makes it convenient. I like its atmosphere as the professors are very supportive of their students. The school is well equipped with resources to advance education. I would prefer if it had more restaurants and theaters to go to after school, but that's not the option, as it is somewhat in the suburbs. Overall, I feel proud to be part of the Bethel committee.

I love the community and atmosphere on the campus! Bethel is an amazing place to meet Christ-centered, loving people, and the small campus allows you to bump into them all the time! I think that this quality makes Bethel a really neat place to live and fellowship with other christians. However one thing I would change would be the dining center food. Often I struggle with eating there due to the lack of options or appetizing meals. However I do really enjoy the Italian and Asian cuisine, due to the fact that it is cooked right in front of you, and seems fresher!
If you're looking for a good Christian school, look elsewhere. This place is a cult. Don't get me wrong I love the friends I've made, but I wouldn't suggest this school to anyone.
I was enrolled during COVID! Things were interesting and a little scary. I did not enjoy the Clique's at the school but LOVED the education, wellness center and diversity of programs offered.

Overall my experience with Bethel has been an emotional rollercoaster of emotions. In the beginning of the semester, I had a hard time adjusting to the dorms and other students. I am one semester in and I can say that I feel at home. I specifically chose this college because it was a christian college and it is somewhere I can grow my faith and also work on my education. As for my education, I haven’t encountered a lot of stress yet and I love my classes. I also love that we have a month where we have one class to focus on. I feel that it helps redirect my focus onto one thing. I love that the campus is small, but has everything that you need. Something that I think Bethel struggles with is their lack of diversity. I believe diversity is a very important thing coming from a minority. Other than that, I haven't come across any more complaints. So far, I am enjoying my college experience and I would love to promote Bethel to others who are looking for a small welcoming environment.
Online school has been the best for me as I am a post-baccalaureate student who just got married and moved to northern MN.

Being at Bethel has made a huge impact on my life. It has grown me to be more of an outgoing person, and learn to connect with people better. I love the community, the programs, for athletics and academics, and I cant wait to see how my life will progress.

Bethel University is located in the St. Paul, MN/Arden Hills, MN area and is a safe campus. Bethel is in a residential area with shopping and restaurants nearby. The campus is beautifully maintained with updated facilities. The athletics are competitive and participate in the MIAC, one of the most highly ranked conferences in the country.
The classes are excellent! Professors are approachable and willing to help and go above and beyond. The Couselors are knowlegeable and engaging. If they do not have an answer, they reach out to the person or department that can answer and solve any questions and/or issues.
Bethel is a Christian Univeristy and has many outlets to learn and grow in your spirituality. There are religion classes, bible studies, Vespers, prayer groups, and church services among other clubs and activities.
I am more than satisified with my experience at Bethel University. I highly recommend attending.
Bethel is a beautiful small campus where everyone is welcome. The class sizes are small. Tuition is high.

ai_summary: Binghamton University, SUNY, stands out for its rigorous academics , particularly in STEM and business programs, which many students praise for their challenge and the quality of education received, often deeming it a "Public Ivy." The campus fosters a vibrant social life with numerous clubs, organizations, and events, although some students feel more could be done to enhance community engagement, particularly for commuters. The dining options receive mixed feedback, with some students calling for improvements in food variety. Professors are generally seen as supportive and engaged, contributing to a collaborative learning environment. While the beautiful campus and community feel are frequently mentioned, some students have expressed a desire for better communication from administration and improvements to campus facilities. Overall, many students find Binghamton to be a valuable choice , balancing quality education with a lively campus experience.

reviews.text: I enjoyed the campus, it’s very beautiful and also walkable! The arena is a very nice event center. The community of students and teachers are all welcoming.

I would definitely recommend Binghamton University. As a history major, I always felt that professors wer committed to being active members of the community and dedicated to ensuring the success and education of students. I was able to find like-minded people and build a balanced life for myself while at Binghamton.

My experience at Binghamton University has been incredibly positive. The academics are challenging and engaging, with professors who genuinely care about student success. I’ve especially appreciated the strong sense of community and the opportunities to get involved through programs, research, and student organizations. The campus environment encourages both personal and academic growth. If I could suggest one improvement, it would be expanding resources and advising for internships and career placement, but overall Binghamton offers a supportive and enriching college experience.
I loved touring this campus! It was so pretty, and I loved the dorms. They are very open to LGBTQ students!
I had a great time at SUNY binghamton and met a lot of great people. A lot of the professors were not too great at teaching so I hope that changes in the future

I'm a transfer student so things are difficult to get into but I've had some problems with the food safety and quality here. I also feel like some of the cleanliness isn't good enough in my opinion. I think the student life is good but it's hard to feel welcomed in some of these spaces. Finding a job here has been a bit difficult, but overall I still think this is a good college that provides pretty good information and academics.
Binghamton University has many good research opportunities and many different clubs. The surrounding area does not have too much to do.

I've loved my experience at Binghamton University over the past few months. I'm a second-semester freshman, and I've found Binghamton extremely welcoming during my time here. The classes are challenging enough to be engaging, but not impossible, and you get a lot of freedom in choosing them (even as a freshman). Binghamton's add-drop period, the first two weeks of the school semester where you can observe other classes and make changes to your schedule, really lets you pick the classes that are best suited for you, from times to teachers, and there are so many classes to chose from. The campus has events almost every day, and there are a variety of clubs and other campus groups to keep students engaged, even if they aren't comfortable going off-campus to explore. I'm very happy with my choice. :)
I would like to see a change in how professors teach. Some of them are focused on meeting requirements the schools/deans set that they forget they have students that need actual learning.

It was good, I’m an out of stater so there isn’t a lot of other people like me. Food is terrible, I gained so much weight and it tastes disgusting. Night life is alright, it’s on the more inexpensive side which is good.

For all the positives Binghamton University has, there are glaring negatives. The academics are rigorous (not in a bad way), and all of the professors I’ve had have more or less been truly knowledgeable and scholars in their chosen field. But thats more or less where it ends. My experience may be different as I’m a non-traditional student and don’t live on campus so I cannot speak to housing, parties, or anything like that, but I can say the food on campus is terrible and they do not accommodate allergies well, the student body is not meaningfully diverse (if you’re from Long Island you may think its diverse — there are a lot of international students but theres no real large BIPOC presence on campus but I will say LGBTQ+ students are well represented) and the city of Binghamton and overall area are horrible.
