I appreciated the supportive faculty and staff, small class sizes, and experiential learning opportunities. The university’s values-driven mission and beautiful campus also stood out. These elements combine to create a nurturing and inspiring environment for students.
Overall, Gwynedd Mercy University provided me with the tools, experiences, and confidence to pursue my goals. I recommend it to anyone seeking a supportive academic environment encouraging personal and professional growth.

The university is very good academically. They have smaller classrooms meaning that you can get to know the professor and feel more comfortable asking questions about the course. There are also tutoring services and many ways to succeed in your courses. There aren't many campus activities, and it is hard to connect with other peers of different majors at Gwynedd Mercy University.
I enjoy Gwynedd Mercy. The professors are here to help you. There is not much of a party scene. Campus life is kind of boring. The food if you are staying on campus is not that good.

My first semester has been great my professors have been very supportive and helpful through all my assignments. The tutor mentors have been also very helpful when needed. I’m also greatful we do not have midterms and tests are not to stressful.

I have started GMU and so far I really like it. The professors are professional and I feel that when I need questions answered or clarification they respond quickly. I like that it’s a small campus. The area is very nice and safe. The dorm room has been redone and it suits its purpose. I’m excited to be part of GMU. So far I have a positive review .

Gwynedd Mercy University provides a Diverse atmosphere where each student can apply their knowledge. The professors are very understanding and take the time to get to know all their students. As an education major, Gwynedd prepares you for the outside world with the future jobs you have. For example, a lot of my assignments are projects that are mainly lesson plans instead of applying our knowledge taking exams. They do a lot of activities in which everyone is invited. Gwynedd is a great college to get to know new people such as friends, professors, and people that can connect you to your future careers.

Gwynedd Mercy University showcases true dedication to providing adequate support for every student to reach success. In many ways, professors have made their time available to help struggling students and provide opportunities for networking, and resume-building events. Since this university is smaller than most, professors and other staff members consistently utilize relationship-building to strengthen their community and have successfully created a space where going to class does not feel like a chore. The atmosphere of student relations has created many instances of positive mental health strategies to support us, students, during stressful times throughout the year. As a school built on the foundation of mercy, giving back to our local community encourages students to take advantage of our communal food pantry, and life skills courses/pathways, and to experience our college years in ways that enrich us to branch out as influential alumni.

ai_summary: Hampton University offers a unique and vibrant college experience with a strong sense of community, particularly celebrated for its historical significance as an HBCU. Students appreciate the supportive faculty , who are generally invested in their academic success, particularly in popular programs like business and nursing. The campus itself is noted for its picturesque setting and rich history, fostering a collaborative environment. Many students remark on the lively student life , complemented by a variety of organizations and events that encourage involvement and cultural pride. However, there are concerns about outdated facilities , particularly housing and dining options, as well as persistent issues with administrative responsiveness. While the academic rigor is generally appreciated, some students express frustration with their experiences navigating financial aid and other services. In terms of social life, while there is a strong school spirit and opportunities for Greek life, students mention a lack of diverse social events compared to other institutions. Overall, Hampton University is seen as an empowering and community-oriented place for personal and academic growth, albeit with areas needing improvement.

reviews.text: As a first-year music education major, I have enjoyed my first year at Hampton University. From high school, my Advanced Placement (AP) and Dual Enrollment credits transferred, allowing me to be a sophomore after only one completed semester. With this school being my dream school, it certainly hasn't disappointed. Being a part of academic groups and the school's band, I've already learned a ton and gained experiences that will last me a lifetime.
I do not believe anyone should go to Hampton because it was falsely advertised as "The Standard of Excellence".

My experience at Hampton University has been truly unforgettable—an environment where culture, community, and excellence show up every day. From the moment I arrived, I felt surrounded by pride, tradition, and a supportive campus spirit that pushes you to grow while staying connected to who you are. The professors have been a standout part of my experience: knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in students—not just in grades, but in confidence, purpose, and long-term success. Whether in class discussions, office hours, or mentorship moments, I’ve felt challenged in the best way and encouraged to aim higher. Hampton has given me more than an education; it’s given me a sense of belonging, motivation, and a deep appreciation for the culture and people who make the university special.

I am a sophomore and majoring in Business Administration. I am a lacrosse player and love the community at Hampton University. The professors and advisors are encouraging and supportive. Hampton has numerous internship opportunities for its students and I am receiving an excellent education within a nurturing environment. Lastly, the dorms are nice enough, but the food offered in the cafeteria is sub-par. I highly recommend Hampton if one is looking for a safe and smaller university!
Love it so far. Your experience is what you make it, and they allow you to take the initiative. Food, housing, administration could be improved.

I am only a freshman, but so far, Hampton is a pretty good experience. They have resources all over for you to take part in, as well as companies that come into the student center that may be of interest to you. The inclusivity is really appreciated and helpful. Some of the professors are very helpful as well and are willing to stay and talk with you during their office hours. Unfortunately, the food has its good and bad days. In the beginning it will seem pretty good, and then as the year goes on, it’ll taste somewhat ok and they’ll sometimes serve the same thing for a week in the buffet line. They have little appreciation days for different cultures and have sweets served on the side. But as an overall experience, Hampton is pretty cool. I plan on exploring more and seeing what else they have to offer me in these next 4 years.

As a first-year student at Hampton University, I am very satisfied. At first, I was nervous because I am 8 hours away from home, but completing my first semester has taught me a lot. I was able to create new bonds and relationships, learn how to network, learn etiquette, experience my first homecoming, and feel welcomed and safe while learning with others who have a lot in common with me. I would say that the professors want all of their students to be successful and recommend resources if needed. Overall, Hampton University was the best decision I've made, and I am glad to say I am a Hampton Pirate.

I love Hampton University because it offers a supportive, creative environment, strong architecture program that allows me to build a real bond with her architecture peers as we grow, design, and push each other creatively. Hampton inspires Black excellence, and a beautiful, historic campus that fuels design imagination and personal growth. I just love living by the sea.

As a third-year student here at Hampton University, I would say my experience has been great. However, as I approach graduation, there is a shift in becoming an upperclassman. Professors start to treat you differently and make you feel like you're not part of the community that you first felt when you got here as a freshman. But I have loved all the opportunities I have had at Hampton University.

I would say my experience with Hampton University so far has been very interesting. I've made a few friends who are quite nice, and I've attended a few events that have been quite fun. Although I do wish a few things were fixed or at least a bit different. For example, I kind of wish the food had more options and wasn't repetitive when it comes to what they serve in the dining hall. I also wish the dorms were maintained a bit better. I've had some weird experiences, but overall, I would say the campus is somewhat nice.

My experience at Hampton has been a journey of growth, connection, and self-discovery. So far I am only in my sophomore year of college. My first year taught me lessons about life and relationships. Hampton is a beautiful school with an even prettier campus. Your experience at school is truly what you make it.

I participated in two, six-week programs over the past two summers and am currently enrolled as a Freshman. I truly enjoyed my time at Hampton over the past couple of months and am looking forward to thriving academically in the months to come.

Attending Hampton University has been a rewarding and transformative experience. One of the things I appreciate most is the strong sense of community and tradition on campus. The culture here encourages leadership, academic excellence, and personal growth. Faculty and staff are generally very supportive, and I’ve had opportunities to connect with mentors who genuinely care about student success. The history and pride of Hampton are also felt everywhere, which creates a motivating environment.
What I’ve really enjoyed is the campus atmosphere—it feels vibrant and alive, especially with events, organizations, and student-led initiatives that bring people together. The diversity of students and the friendships I’ve built have been invaluable.
Overall, Hampton has given me a sense of pride and belonging, and I’m grateful for the experiences and growth I’ve had here.

Hampton University was an experience like none other. As a 2014 grad, I feel like I was there at the perfect time where the tradition and culture of the university was still being honored, however, conversations regarding moving the school forward were taking place. I loved being at a school where while everyone looked like me, there was still so much diversity of background and experience. HBCU's are full of culture and Hamptons culture shines bright. Since my graduation I have always said, that I am not sure what was more important to me, the education that I received or the network that I created.

I've enjoyed my experience thus far. I have made new friends and found myself immersed in different interactive clubs and activities. Also, I can get good exercise as there is a lot of walking. There is a basketball gym that I can go to freely. The food can vary. Soul foul, Thursday always brings my mood up, though. I do hope that the campus can improve some of the housing conditions, as the rooms are smaller. I am looking to further my experience at the illustrious Hampton University.

ai_summary: Hardin-Simmons University (HSU) is often described as a close-knit community where students feel welcomed and supported. Many reviews highlight the strong relationships between students and faculty, emphasizing the small class sizes that foster personal connections and individual attention. The campus is noted for its beauty and inviting atmosphere , contributing to a sense of home for many students. Academic programs are generally well-regarded, especially in fields like nursing and social work, where professors are praised for their approachability and dedication. However, some students express concerns about the school's diversity, campus activities, and the town of Abilene being somewhat limiting for young adults. While most reviewers appreciate the faith-based environment , there are mentions of a need for more openness regarding differing beliefs, particularly related to the LGBTQ community. Overall, HSU offers a supportive educational experience, although it may not be the ideal fit for everyone, particularly those seeking a more diverse or urban setting.

reviews.text: My overall experience with Hardin-Simmons University is that it is expensive to go but I'm makin the of it honestly

Campus is so pretty, you can never go wrong chilling on the second floor of the library! With a smaller campus your able to be in class sizes that professors are easier to manage and you have every opportunity to communicate directly with your professor if something isn't clicking, or just to have someone to talk to! There is always something going on while your on campus, whether it's hosted by SABERS (student event planners on campus) or just fun out on by the general student, there never a dull moment when your on campus!!
I liked the living on my own away from my home but not to far away! Football allows me to focus what I love while getting my degree.
Hardin Simmons overall provided me a good education with great professors. I did not appreciate the lack of diversity nor support for students who are different than their current demographic.

Hardin-Simmons is a great university, especially if you desire a small school with high quality education. Because the student population is small, you get a lot of one on one time with professors, get to know them and ask more questions.

At Hardin-Simmons, it is nice to know everyone in my class and also know my professor in a closer relationship and is not just a teacher to me. The sports are amazing and the campus life is pretty good I love everything about Hardin-Simmons.

This University is a great place if you like a smaller environment. I personally like it and most people are easy to talk to. Hardin-Simmons gives plenty of resources to students who might struggle with school. They also put on community events for students to have fun at. They also encourage going to different athletic games for support of the student athletes. You have to have 80 chapel credits before you graduate but they make it easy to get. They have 2 chapel services per week and provide a way to get them at FCA or doing community service. HSU is a great community for connecting and learning. Class sizes are small so it’s easier to get help from your professors who actually care about your education and want you to succeed.
Hardin- Simmons is a great place to school at. Very calm, great professors and would 100% recommend.
I've been enjoying my time here at Hardin-Simmons. Although I can't say much about other programs, Hardin-Simmons has great music programs with professors who care about their students.

Hardin-Simmons is great if you like being closely connected with smaller-sized group of students and professors! The staff and campus make you feel at home, and the coursework makes sure you're not just learning but involved too. I don't think I'd like to see anything changed. The people here are friendly, the atmosphere is great, and the pace of life is great for whatever you're participating in!

Personally I just got accepted into Hardin university this week it is my senior year of high school and I've heard great things about this university and would love to attend but I need help to do that I want to major in physcolgy so I can then earn my degree and start working as a counselor or as a teacher who he's children/teens get through things they should not have been going through at a young age cause personally myself I have went through some things as a kid that make me to way I am now, I had to figure out my life by myself and I want to be there for these kids who don't have anybody I want to help as many children as I am but I need you help to earn this scholarship so I can have the money to go to school this sis my dream and I want to pursue it

Hardin-Simmons feels like home. Campus is small enough to easily get around. Class sizes are small so you are more than just a number with your professors. I currently play baseball there and overall love the area and school.

Hardin-Simmons was an amazing place to attend. The best part of HSU is the people! Faculty and staff care a lot about the students and helping them achieve their goals and learn, and the other students are great at making others feel welcome. Overall, Hardin-Simmons University seems to offer a unique and personalized educational experience, but it may not be the best fit for everyone ³.

I am in my second semester of school, but as an art major I am so excited to continue attending Hardin-Simmons. The art walk and multiple museums in Abilene are really great opportunities for me to network and see other artists styles and process. I also joined the concert band and the music we play is so cool and the people are very nice.
What I like about Hardin-Simmons is that the classes aren't big. Not only are the classes small but the campus is as well. It's the right size for me if I'm honest.

ai_summary: Harding University is often described as a welcoming, tight-knit community that emphasizes both spiritual and academic growth. Many students appreciate the strong relationships they build with professors who genuinely care about their success and well-being, creating a supportive educational environment . The campus itself is noted for its beauty and is active with various social clubs and intramural sports that enhance student life. However, some students find the rules and conservative culture to be restrictive, which can affect their overall experience, especially for those from more urban backgrounds. Overall, Harding seems to provide a distinct blend of rigorous academics with a faith-centered approach that encourages personal development, though some students express a desire for more diversity and leniency in regulations.

reviews.text: My experience at Harding has been great. I believe it is the right place for me. It has such a welcoming feeling, and people here are very kind and polite. This is a Christian school, and I am a Christian. The environment is very God-Centered which I love. Most people here love Jesus in some way. That goes to say about their personalities, and why I feel so welcomed here. The professors are great! The academics are very strong. The dorms are really nicea, and the open campus is incredibly beautiful.
Absolute dream college! The professors here are so down to earth, and the campus is beautiful! The students and peers are usually quite friendly and kind, too.

It has been a wonderful experience at Harding. The students and the Harding staff are all supportive and kind. They even have a great athletic department with ranking sports teams. It is located on a small community with many things to do outdoors within a short drive.

I love Harding!! I am not in a social club, but I still have a wide friend group. My suite is amazing, my professors actually care about progress, and the overall culture is real. Part of why I picked Harding was because people smiled and said "hi" to me while on my visitor tour. The tour guide ran into her friends all over campus. It was so welcoming compared to other schools I toured. If you are on the fence, just come and see the place. It is not for everyone, but the people who want to be here adore it.
Parents - please don't force your kids to come here. If they do not want to be here, they will make that known. The other students can tell.
Great school, well respected for Cybersecurity. Close-nit student body. I d like to see more diversity in the programs at the school.

I liked Harding University’s commitment to following Biblical principles and encouraging students in their faith journey with Christ. Campus life has the option to join clubs, which is Harding’s equivalent of a fraternity/sorority, but more tame in my opinion. I did not personally join one, but from an outside perspective, I would recommend it to anyone who enjoys social scenes like that in college.
Love it here! Amazing professors, great social experience, and decent food. Would highly reccomend to anyone looking for a safe, amazing college.

Harding University is my home away from home. After my very first visit here last year, I knew this place was different than every other university I had visited. The people are all amazing, kind, and welcoming. I love all of the opportunities for social clubs, faith formation, and athletic involvement. This school just feels like home and it feels like I am a part of something amazing! I love Harding University for the strong academics, the team bible studies, the amazing friendships, the beautiful campus, and the chapel devotions. You should definitely check out Harding, and you'll fall in love with this school!

I really like the individuals here at Harding University. Everyone here is so kind and are always there to lend a helping hand. It’s a little harder for me to speak about some of the on campus activities and what not because I am an athlete here and don’t have a whole lot of down time. When I do get to spend time with others here I really enjoy it and am so thankful that I ended up here in Searcy.
I love attending Harding University. The education is great and I know that my professors truly care. In addition, I love that I am getting an education from a Christian perspective.
I had a good time within the theatre program there, but Harding doesn't have as much going on as other universities would
My experience with Harding University has been good. I appreciate the knowledge of the professors and the care they take to make sure us students are getting the material.

It's an incredible place both visually and physically. The staff is so nice and helpful and students are always willing to help and learn. The town surrounding the university is fully behind it as well.
I have had a phenomenal experience. Every professor is interested in their students' longterm success, and their care and attention is clear and evident.
Harding is such a unique, fun environment and area to be in. The people, local and international are so friendly…. The campus is beautiful with some things to do around town to keep you busy!

ai_summary: Harvard University offers a dynamic and diverse academic environment , characterized by rigorous courses and distinguished faculty who engage students in meaningful discussions. Many reviews highlight the campus's beautiful architecture and convenient resources, including libraries and student centers, which foster collaboration and community. While the academic challenge is noted as significant, students appreciate the opportunities for personal growth and networking that come with being part of such a prestigious institution. Conversely, some reviews point to a less vibrant social scene , with concerns about inclusivity for marginalized groups and a perceived sense of elitism on campus. Overall, Harvard attracts students seeking both academic excellence and a unique college experience, though navigating its social landscape can be complex.

reviews.text: It was a good school with strong academic programs, social life, and on-campus housing. Appreciated the diversity in student population as well

When I first applied to this school, I was elated. It had always been a fantasy of mine to attend THE Harvard University. until I realized that my dream had became a NIGHTMARE. Each night I would be awoken from my sleep by the feeling of dirty RATS clambering all over me. The worst part? One of the rats had RABIES and I had to go to the DOCTOR and get a rabies shot: which led to the continuation of my SEVERE mental health issues and SEWERSLIDAL thoughts. Thinking about coming here? Thinking about relishing in the utter prestige of the university. You're better off going to COMMUNITY COLLEGE. In fact, you're better off packing all your bags, and signing up for MILITARY SERVICE. World war III here I come! ANYTHING is better than Harvard. At least on the battlefields of Iran I won't have to get a RABIES SHOT.
It was aight. .

> “My overall experience at Harvard University has been life‑changing. The academic rigor and world‑renowned faculty challenged me to expand my intellectual horizons every day. I especially appreciated the cutting‑edge research facilities and the vibrant student organizations that foster leadership and innovation. The diverse community made the campus feel like a global hub, enriching both my social and academic life. On the flip side, I felt the course registration system could be more flexible for interdisciplinary interests, and there’s room to improve mentorship programs for graduate students. Despite these tweaks, Harvard equipped me with skills and confidence to pursue ambitious goals. I recommend prospective students to embrace the intense environment and make the most of the unparalleled

It is a nice school with lots of mind blowing structures. Based on my analysis, it has produced a set of brilliant students who are out there appreciating the good instil of discipline into them which made them what they're now

My overall experience with Harvard University has been outstanding. The academic environment is challenging and inspiring, with professors who are leaders in their fields and truly invested in student success. Campus resources, from libraries to research labs, are world-class and always accessible. The diverse student body creates a vibrant, supportive community where collaboration is encouraged and new ideas are welcomed. There are countless opportunities for involvement, whether through clubs, events, or internships. Harvard not only pushed me to excel academically, but also helped me grow personally and professionally. I feel truly privileged to be a part of such an amazing institution.

Everything about Harvard was wonderful, except for the administration and the people in charge of running the school. They could do a lot better at adjusting policies with the times, listening to student advocates and their feedback, and taking care of the student body's mental health. They simply worry too much about their statistics and liability. Otherwise, the professors, the people, the plethora of diverse classes, and the housing/campus life is amazing!

My experience at Harvard University has been a mix of awe, growth, challenge, and perspective. It’s one of those places where the mythology surrounding it - the prestige, the legacy, the brand meets reality in unexpected ways. Yes, it lives up to its reputation for excellence, but what most people don’t realise is that Harvard’s real strength isn’t its name, its red brick buildings, or even its professors. It’s the ecosystem a living, breathing network of ambitious, curious, and sometimes intimidatingly accomplished people who push you to think, question, and stretch yourself every single day. Harvard isn’t just a college; it’s almost like an experiment in human potential. You walk into classrooms where debates feel like mini-United Nations sessions, dining halls buzz with the next startup pitch, and hallways echo with the sound of students juggling 10 ideas at once. It’s inspiring and exhausting at the same time like trying to drink from a firehose, but somehow loving the chaos of it.

My experience with Harvard University has been highly inspiring and academically enriching. The intellectual environment pushes students to think critically, explore interdisciplinary ideas, and aim for excellence. Professors are deeply knowledgeable and encourage independent thinking, while the resources, libraries, and research opportunities are exceptional. The diverse student community offers exposure to different perspectives from around the world. However, the academic pressure can be intense at times, and better mental health support and workload balance would improve the overall experience. Overall, Harvard provides an unmatched learning environment that prepares students for leadership and innovation.
It was way too easy. i felt that it couldve have been easier and harder. some classes i felt were so freaking challenging and others were so easy.
As a graduate student, I feel a bit left out. The school definitely prioritizes undergrads. My friend at Yale has a much better experience with the grad school there than I have had here
Most amazing university in the world. Best Ivy League I have attended after Columbia. Especially HMS.

The Aspire Leadar program was my amazing experience I took part in All activities of the program, during my two months there, I worked with people who were in the leadership program. So I could tell you a little about what it's like. This program provides us opportunity of Networking with such students who are away from my country. The amazing thing for me is that the Indian and Pakistani interconnect through this program without discrimination as the Students of one platform. Further Sometimes you are given cross business rotations so you learn how to work in different industries and environments,During my short time as an intern I got to work with some brilliant people and I got an insight into the actual management of an MNC. It is such exposure that trains the employees to face real world situations in the industry, something that isn't given too much attention during undergraduate studies At least this program they did take students who had just finished their graduations

I did a semester as a undergraduate. Besides it being "Harvard" I actually liked it alot. Though it was hard socially. You need to apply and go to interviews for almost every club. So it was hard to make a friendgroup. Though maybe it would be different if a spent a longer time here, idk.

My experience with Harvard University has been both challenging and rewarding. The CORe program is providing a rigorous, practical introduction to business fundamentals, and the case-based learning made the material highly applicable to real-world scenarios.
What I valued most is engaging with a diverse global cohort. The discussions offered fresh perspectives and deepened my learning. The platform itself was intuitive, and the teaching quality lived up to Harvard’s reputation.
If I could suggest one improvement, it would be to expand networking opportunities beyond discussion boards—perhaps through regional meetups or virtual sessions with faculty. This would make the experience feel even more connected to Harvard’s wider community.
Overall, CORe is an excellent experience that I would recommend to anyone seeking to strengthen their business knowledge while connecting with professionals worldwide.

ai_summary: Hofstra University offers a supportive and engaging academic environment , particularly praised for its dedicated professors and smaller class sizes that allow for personal connections and networking opportunities. Students generally find the community welcoming and friendly, with diverse clubs and activities that foster social interaction, although some commuters feel less included in campus life. The university's proximity to New York City enhances internship prospects, particularly in fields like business and communication. However, reviews suggest that tuition is high for the education provided, and while there are commendable academic resources, some amenities, like food options and housing, could be improved. Overall, Hofstra is seen as a vibrant and diverse institution that feels like home to many students, despite its challenges.

reviews.text: Hofstra was a great place for undergrad, as I came in with very little direction but left with concrete goals and objectives. I also made lifelong friends at Hofstra.

Hofstra Univerisity is a great site for networking and creating profound connections but you have to put the work and be willing to seek for resources because everyone wants to get a spot but not everyone gets it.

My overall experience at Hofstra University was positive. The campus is beautiful, and its proximity to New York City gave me great access to internships and real-world opportunities. Most professors were supportive and knowledgeable, especially in smaller classes where it was easier to build strong academic relationships. I also appreciated the wide range of clubs and events that made it easy to get involved and feel part of the community.
However, some areas could improve. Tuition is high, and communication from financial services can be unclear or slow. A few administrative processes, like registration and housing, were more complicated than they needed to be.
Overall, Hofstra provides a strong academic environment with valuable opportunities for growth if you take advantage of campus resources.
As a commuter I do not spend much time on campus or around the social life, but the teachers are good and the area is ok

Hofstra is a nice school and pretty open to a lot of people dynamics and perspectives with a nice atmosphere however it is a college so the food is expensive the assignments are lowkey kinda torture and for some reason they have a bunch of statues around campus depicting different things unique but like if I handed in an assignment that diverse I would so not pass if you get my vibe but it’s a nice college

I am currently a student at Hofstra University and am enjoying my experience. The student event are usually good even though sometimes they aren't planned or advertised well enough. I am glad I chose Hofstra, my favorite part is how pretty the campus is and the proximity to NYC. My least favorite part is the price of the university and the surrounding town is unsafe.

Hofstra is a great college. I would love to go there. It offers an excellent physician assistant program. My future goals is to be in the healthcare field and help others like myself a Bangladeshi American student. I’ve seen my Karen’s persist the tough being an immigrant in New York. When I become a physician assistant I will help other just like me.

My overall experience with Hofstra is both good and bad. One thing that has been good is that I am ahead in credits so I am graduating early. Also, since Hofstra offers an advanced dual degree program, I will be able to graduate two years early with my masters than if I were to go to a different college. However, the program within the dual degree program is a mess. All the classes are messed up, there is a lack of structure and guidelines, and no one in charge seems to know whats going on.

The proximately to NYC really improves the appeal of the school. Like all universities, some professors are amazing, and others are not. There's an overflow of housing of all types currently, which makes it relatively easy to find the accommodations you prefer. The party scene is small but it exists.

What I love to see about Hofstra's campus is how welcoming and sweet everyone is. The community is a little diverse, and goes each day, but it is nice to see everyone connecting and not having to worry about the issues of discrimination occurring.

During my time as a student at Hofstra University, I had an experience that truly shaped both my personal and academic growth. The campus community was welcoming and vibrant, creating an environment where I felt supported from the start. Opportunities such as hands-on clinical experiences and access to well-equipped facilities helped me apply what I learned in real-world settings, building my confidence as a future professional. Overall, Hofstra provided an engaging, supportive atmosphere that encouraged me to challenge myself, develop leadership skills, and prepare for the next steps in my career.

Some of the teachers are amazing, and my overall experience has been amazing and I've grown and learned a lot. I am vegetarian/semi-vegan though and have to get most of my food off campus cause there are no options and not nearly enough to get actual sustenance.
So far I have lived it at Hofstra. I have met so many amazing people and had great opportunities for internships and other things like that through Hofstra’s partnerships.

Hofstra was the best college for me at the time. Yes, it was expensive, but it was worth it to me. The food wasn’t always great, but there were enough options for me to always try something new. Because of this school, I’ve made lifelong friends that I’ll forever be grateful to have met.

-quality of education is really low for the insane tuition and housing costs
-food is extremely overpriced and disgusting, and small in portions
-people always chew super loud and obnoxiously in the library
-dorms are cramped, nasty, and floormates are loud and obnoxious
-really lame parties and student life
-admin won't help you and will be really frustrating
-the area around Hofstra is very unsafe, completely unwalkable, and depressing and dirty

ai_summary: Howard University offers a vibrant community , particularly noted for its strong emphasis on African-American culture and diversity. Many students highlight the strong sense of belonging and familial atmosphere on campus, enriched by meaningful connections with peers from various backgrounds. Academically, the success of the experience largely depends on individual effort and professor engagement, with smaller class sizes in advanced courses facilitating closer relationships with faculty. However, common frustrations arise regarding administrative inefficiencies , particularly in financial aid and student services, which can impact the overall experience. While the school is lauded for its opportunities and rich history, there are notable concerns about organization and the physical state of campus facilities, particularly dining options and housing. Despite these challenges, many students express a deep pride in attending Howard and a belief in the university's potential for personal and professional growth.

reviews.text: My experince has been wondeful. I have met amazing people and who strive for the greatest in life. Howard as challenge me to search and work hard for the things that I want, while also challengeing my determination and grit in success.

Overall, my experience at Howard has been pretty good. The student life is very lively and there is always something going on.
There are some areas for improvement such as housing notification. Our housing notifications for the upcoming school year do not come out until mid summer, giving students and families little time to get a plan if they did not receive housing from Howard.

My overall experience with Howard University has been very meaningful and impactful. One of the things I appreciate most about Howard is its strong sense of community and cultural pride. Being surrounded by motivated students and faculty who genuinely care about Black excellence and leadership creates an inspiring environment. The campus history and traditions also make you feel like you’re part of something bigger than yourself. Howard has a powerful alumni network, and there are many events, guest speakers, and career resources that help students prepare for life after graduation.
One thing I would like to see improve is communication and administrative efficiency. Sometimes processes like registration, financial aid, or housing can be confusing or slow. Improving those systems would make the student experience much smoother.
Overall, Howard is a place that challenges you to grow academically, professionally, and personally.
My experience at Howard University has been very rewarding. Being at Howard there is a sense of healthly competitions that allows you to stay on your toes and become your best self.

As a student who has been incolved since my very first semester and pledged my sophomoreyea I believe I have a great network and perspective of my school. It is very diverse and has and amazing atmosphere withing the campus and the students. The surrounding area is in the northwest are of DC and has some very nice areas and alot of local housing around the area as DC is a condensed city. The professors can oftentimes been from other countries and may cause language barriers within their explaining as english is not always their first language. and the administration can be nice or rude depending on the day and the financial aid office is constantly talked about in issues that happen with the university and can make it hard for you to get holds off your account and often times seem as if they do not want to do their job.

Howard University offers a powerful blend of rich history, academic rigor, cultural pride, and community support that shapes not just students’ education, but their sense of purpose. From the moment you step on campus, you become part of a legacy — one rooted in excellence, resilience, and leadership. Founded in 1867, Howard is known as a premier Historically Black University where academic achievement and social impact go hand in hand.
Academically, Howard challenges students through rigorous coursework across disciplines — from STEM and health sciences to humanities, business, and the arts. Professors are deeply invested in student success, often blending mentorship with high expectations. The classroom environment encourages critical thinking, debate, and real-world application. At the same time, there’s ample support through tutoring, advising, research opportunities, and collaborative projects that empower students to grow and succeed.

Overall, I truly cherish my time at Howard University. I am very blessed to be surrounded by the friends that I made from the moment I stepped onto campus. Academics have been treating me right as well. The accounting community is small but mighty, and the director wants nothing but for me to succeed as a young Black woman. My experience at Howard has been amazing so far, and I'm so grateful that I can call this school my home.
Howard is a great experience for anyone that has ambition and a desire to be great in life. Everything is good at the campus.
It's been great so far, I've had a good amount of professional opportunities and I've made some good friends so far.

Howard is a good college. There are some places they can improve. I can only speak in reference to the architecture department. More professors need to be available; there's courses that are made that do not come with a professor. They need more scholarship options for students. Howard gets a lot of donations and grants; some portion of that money should go to the students. I feel as though after admissions you are left alone for your rest of your time at Howard. But the student life is very fun!

I'm currently attending Howard, and my favorite part of going here is campus life. Meeting new people from all walks of life has truly been rewarding. I would like to see the financial aid opportunities become more abundant. The financial aid employees here tend to give you the runaround.
Howard university is a d1 and r1 campus, allowing for a wide variety of experiences and opprutunities for students, reguardless of interest and desired professional outcomes.

My experience at Howard University has been both challenging and rewarding. What I have liked most is the strong sense of community and purpose that exists on campus. Howard fosters an environment where students are encouraged to strive for excellence while also supporting one another academically, culturally, and personally. I have especially appreciated the dedication of faculty and staff who are invested in student success and who push us to think critically and grow beyond the classroom. Being at an institution with such a rich legacy has motivated me to work harder and take pride in my education.
One change I would like to see is increased access to academic and wellness resources, particularly during high-stress periods in the semester. Expanding tutoring availability, advising hours, and mental health support would further enhance the student experience.
Howard is a great place for networking as the Alumni & Companies are abundant here, as they visit a lot. It's the #1 producer of jobs for students
I thoroughly enjoyed my experience! Everyday is there is truly something new going on on campus from events, organizations and opportunities to network and connect. I absolutely love y HBCU

ai_summary: Husson University is characterized by its small campus and close-knit community , making it easy for students to form connections with peers and professors alike. Reviewers frequently mention the strong academic support offered through small class sizes, which allows for personalized attention from instructors who are generally described as knowledgeable and approachable. Students appreciate the various student life activities organized on campus, although some feel there could be more options, especially on weekends. The campus culture is described as welcoming, with a focus on inclusivity and support across diverse backgrounds. Many students also highlight the quality of the dining services at Husson, although there are mixed opinions regarding financial aid transparency and some academic offerings. Overall, students feel that Husson provides a conducive environment for both personal and academic growth.

reviews.text: This is a small school which is helpful for student who prefer smaller communities. The staff and security officers are kind and make the school a safe environment. The food in the dining hall needs to have better quality, the meet isn't the greatest. I do enjoy the variety that they've added to the convenient stores on campus.
Ive requested information multiple times and have yet to recieve any communications from them regarding their school.
My overall experience has been quite good. I've enjoyed living on campus and having access to the on-campus studios, although some of my neighbors can be quite loud late into the night.

I have had a great experience with Husson. I had the opportunity to join Husson's golf team this past fall, and it has been an amazing experience, from the guys on the team to the coaches and to the facility itself. It was sad last winter when some students found out their program had been cut. Many of my friends had to either switch majors or schools. However, my experience with the school has been good. The nursing program is thriving and has lots of students who are on track to graduate early.
As a first year student. I am trying to focus on class work and still be social I have joined a sorority and feel that my first semester is great
I love how accessible the online courses are, how easy to work with the professors are, and how engaging the classes are. I’ve learned so much and done some important networking already!

Husson is a great school when it comes to academics. If you want to succeed in a specific career field, come to Husson. They offer numerous undergrad and graduate programs that are highly rated, and the rate of employment after graduation is extremely high. Other than academics the rest is fairly lackluster. The food is good, the dorms are okay, and the professors can be a little arrogant. The Bangor area is nice in the summer, but there aren't many places to have fun around here. If you don't like small, high-school like classes, do not come here either. Attendance is mandatory in many classes. Personally, I enjoy Husson. I have made a lot of friends and have really learned a lot. It's a little pricy, and can be boring sometimes, but I can get over that if it means I can reach my goals.
Unfortunately not a transparent university. They will screw you over to get the most money out of you and will misrepresent aid and FASFA available to get you in the door.

My experience at Husson has been excellent. I really love the Canvas app they use for online students. Online school can be tough, but having an app that’s easy to manage, and professors who are willing to help, has made my stress so much less than it could’ve been.
Super friendly staff and easy to communicate with! They have excellent degree options and offer many different funding options as well. Overall so far my experience has been really good!
very friendly, thorough group of team members at the college allowing students to prosper in their academic fields.

I've loved every minute of my time at Husson. Espeically, as a baseball player for the last 4 years it was an unbelievable time. I've never felt so close to some people, and have found a place that I'm proud to call home.
Love the small campus. Love that everything needed is close to campus- you don't have to go far. Great sports teams! I would recommend Husson University!

So far, I feel like I have found a home here at Husson. The professors do all they can to make sure that you succeed. They are also personable and have great humor. The education program has already made me feel excited and ready to go into the classroom. They make sure you get into a classroom right away; this helps you realize if teaching is what you want to do. The clubs are also fun. I'm a part of the theater program here. Everyone has been so kind, open, and make sure you feel like you belong. If I could go back, I would choose Husson a million times over again!
Husson is a small school. Your farthest class is 5 minutes from ur dorm. There’s a bunch of parking. They let freshman bring their cars.

ai_summary: At Idaho State University, students appreciate the welcoming and inclusive community , highlighting the supportive nature of faculty and staff who genuinely care about their academic success. The campus is noted for its safe environment and active student life , with various extracurricular activities and events that foster social engagement. While many reviews commend the strong academic programs and helpful advisors, some students express concerns about organization, communication, and the quality of on-campus dining options. The campus's size strikes a balance for students, offering a traditional college experience while maintaining a more personal atmosphere. The affordability of ISU is frequently mentioned as a positive aspect, alongside its location in Pocatello, which offers some recreational opportunities, although some find the local area lacking in activities. Overall, ISU emerges as a solid option for those seeking a supportive community and diverse academic programs.

reviews.text: I went to high school in the general area. Idaho State University is viewed as a lower prestige but affordable option for college. Professors are really hit or miss. A handful are the best teachers I've ever had, but for others I'm astonished that they're allowed to teach. There's a pretty wide selection of courses and majors to choose from, and overall I'd say the education is pretty good quality. Life on campus can be pretty dead unfortunately, but there's a lot to do in town.

I love the education I’m receiving. I have had a few hiccups at the end of my bachelors education with professor, though overall I had a great experience and highly recommend ISU to anyone! For an online program, they do well, but some of the fully asynchronous classes are difficult because the lectures are either not great or are nonexistent. Which makes learning more difficult.

Overall I’ve had a pretty positive experience at Idaho State University so far! I love the classes and the people I have met! I also love the activities that the school has provided they are super fun!

So far Idaho State University has given me everything that I wanted from a university; they have great professors that truly care about you and that know the material by heart. They also have amazing program like the Honors program that help create schedules and make sure you are on the right path which for a first generation student is an amazing resource to have!
ISU was great, my overall time there was very comfortable and I gained an education to make my future successful. The only thing I struggled with was parking near any of my classes.

I wish the class regimen had a more consistent approach for students to align their schedules without having huge time gaps between classes. Also, the availability of food choices is very limited. I would like to see breakfast options at different places and times. There are many doctorate level professors who unabashedly use bad language and curse words in their regular lecture sessions. Higher learning is supposed to be just that, higher learning, not gutter-snipe demeaning words that detract from the facts and truths being presented.

There are a couple of professors to avoid, but other than that the experience on campus is pretty great. I love all of my classes and especially enjoy the labs that they have here. Food anywhere other than turner is fantastic, turner has some good options, but they aren’t always good. I have been living on campus in Rendezvous which I recommend if you can get in your housing application early enough to get in. Housing is interesting here at ISU… Some buildings are significantly better than others. The South dorms are definitely less developed than housing such as Rendezvous, Bengal Creek, and Schubert Heights. Overall, my experience here has been really good and there are tons of resources for students. We have a good tutoring program and advisors are available so you can talk to someone if you have any questions or issues.
So far, ISU has been a great campus to study at. The only concerns I have ever had are that there aren’t many good options available, and they are expensive at that.
Idaho state university has an amazing athletics program with amazing coaches, amazing trainers and an amazing president of the university.

So far I really enjoy my academic experience on campus. It is very walkable and is in a good area where there is a lot of nature but also a lot of things to do and places to eat if you don't want on campus stuff. I've really enjoyed my classes and proffesors, though there is a lot of construction on campus which makes it hard to sometimes pay attention in classes.

Taking online courses through Idaho State University has been a breeze. The professors make you feel like you are in the classroom with them, they are very great at their jobs. I have learned so much through this university, and I couldn't recommend it enough.
I've only taken one semester so far, but it's been an excellent time studying, the professors are very understanding, communicative, and helpful.

My experience at Idaho State University has been really good so far. The campus feels friendly and welcoming, and my professors have been super helpful and understanding. I’ve met a lot of nice people, and it’s been easy to get involved in things I’m interested in. I also like how there are a lot of resources for students who need help adjusting to college or keeping up with classes.
If I could change one thing, I’d want more food options on campus and maybe a few more fun events to help students connect outside of class. But overall, I really like ISU and feel like it’s a great place to start my college journey in psychology.

I loved attending Idaho State. The professors are genuinely concerned with your progression towards a degree. They are interested in your success. Working with academic advisors greatly impacted the courses I took and the success I had each semester.
I am an online student, and everything was well done. Streamlined application, veteran/military services available, and friendly advisors.

ai_summary: At the Illinois Institute of Technology, students often highlight the strong academic environment , particularly in STEM disciplines, with many noting the dedication of professors and the rigorous curriculum . The campus fosters a small community feel, making it easier to form connections, though some students feel that the social life can be lacking and there's a perception of it being quieter compared to larger universities. Many students appreciate the diverse population , which includes a significant number of international students, contributing to varied perspectives and experiences. While the proximity to downtown Chicago provides ample opportunities for extracurricular activities, concerns about campus safety and the quality of facilities, including housing and dining, are commonly mentioned. Additionally, students have called for improvements in career services and job placement support.

reviews.text: The Illinois Institute of Technology been a great experience for me, especially because I'm not originally from Chicago. There are tons of unique students from lots of different backgrounds, and I feel safe on campus even though it's in the heart of Chicago.

My overall experience is good, the academics at IIT is great and it’s very easy to fit in and to also find help and maneuver through college easyily. The only thing I would say is there isn’t much diversity at all. I’m African American and there isn’t many at IIT and majority of other races move as a group and it’s hard to find your “group”

At Illinois Institute of Technology, I believe a student has no shortage of career and academic opportunities for students. One could go around and find many opportunities for on campus jobs, or ask around for an internship or research opportunity. Plus the school's Elevate program is said to have many of the students find jobs and careers in their field as soon as they get out college. And even if you don't get a job right away, they allow you to continue school, with some charge, towards a master's or PHD. And as well help you with essay review and resume completion. The classes or courses are sometimes hard and challenging, but it is because the class covers so much content and actually teaches what is the most important to cover. However, what the school has in rigorous classes and career opportunities, it seems to lack in social life. The content in the classes is quite a lot to cover and review, so many students just focus on preparing for exams. However, it depends on the person

Overall, I'm happy at Illinois Tech. I play sports, so that is the reason I decided to complete my education at Illinois Tech. There were only a few issues. My dorm room my Freshman and Sophomore years was littered with mold. I was sick a lot and finally, after getting an upper GI and colonoscopy to rule out other issues, I opened up my air conditioner to find a ton of mold in there. They did come in and clean it up, which was great, so I won't fault them 100% because it was taken care of. The professors are good - I'm happy with them so far. Each one wants to help you succeed. The campus is clean and safe. I don't ever have to be to concerned.

I am currently a graduate student in IIT. I finished one semester, and have three more. I really love the depth of the courses offered here. Especially as a grad student in computer science, the lectures go in detail, making in genuinely interesting to go to class every day. The professors I've had so far have been incredibly helpful in making things easier for the students, be it explaining concepts again, assignments, etc. I would recommend this program 100% for anyone who has love for computer science and won't mind being and participating in challenging courses.
Overall, not a bad school to graduate from as it has good reputation, but not up to the expectations and some professors should not be qualified to teach so many classes.

I really do not recommend the school. The professors here do indeed came from great institutions as their background. However, THEY DO NOT KNOW HOW TO TEACH OR PASS ON KNOWLEDGE. Some professors will INTENTIONALLY fail you so you would retake the class. The school cannot do anything about this as they follow the law "academic freedom" which the professors are the only ones who get to fail or succeed you even if it is based on wrongful and biased decisions. It is ranked the most depressed university in the country. It is the most unsafe as it is in SOUTH SIDE OF CHICAGO. The school is extremely dirty and run down. I do not recommend this school. There are better schools with better academic experiences.
It's a good college if you are strict on pursuing a STEM field. The professors are good and the surrounding Chicago area is also.
Amazing architecture and engineering school. Not a very strong party scene. would recommend for serious students that want to innovate and get connections set up in Chicago

Illinois Institute of Technology is a small campus with limited activities, but I really appreciate the academics. Many professors are passionate and care deeply about their students, making the learning experience strong. However, some improvements are needed. Several buildings are outdated, with broken auditorium seats and water leaks when it rains. The food on campus is just okay, but I wish there were more dining options nearby, as choices are limited. Another issue is the gym—it’s small, overcrowded, and lacks air conditioning, which makes it uncomfortable. Overall, IIT offers quality education, but the facilities need significant upgrades to match.
It seems pretty lonely and many students I've spoken with have said that it can get pretty depressing at times and that most people tend to be introverted and keep to themselves.

academic environment as challenging but rewarding. professors are passionate, knowledgeable, and experienced professionals in their fields, providing students with valuable real-world insights. Illinois Tech is a robust research environment with opportunities for students to participate in cutting-edge projects and gain hands-on experience. Campus life is academically focused, with many clubs and activities centered around major-related interests. Illinois Tech over all is dedicated to preparing students for successful careers, offering resources such as career fairs and resume support.

Illinois Institute of Technology is expensive and does not have the facilities to show it. The academics are quality for the most part, but the buildings and food services are not worth the cost. The expensive apartment dorms had cockroaches, the cheap ones had fire alarm activations upsettingly often and they are loud. Many academic and student buildings were leaking. The food program is low quality and in my time at the school they discontinued the most popular/workable meal plan which allowed students to attend the somewhat-better restaurant on campus.
I enjoy my academic journey in college in many aspects. I have a great time my mates in group sections, lectures, and college life around the campus.

Illinois Institute of Technology is an outstanding university that combines academic excellence, innovation, and a truly global outlook. The campus is both historic and forward-looking, creating an inspiring environment for driven, curious students. Professors are not only knowledgeable but also approachable and deeply committed to student success. IIT offers top-tier resources in engineering, computer science, and architecture, along with abundant research opportunities starting as early as freshman year. What sets IIT apart is its collaborative spirit and strong focus on diversity and inclusion. It’s a place where critical thinking is nurtured, and real-world problem-solving is encouraged. Students don’t just learn theory—they build, design, and create. IIT is a future-facing university that never loses sight of the people it serves.

ai_summary: At Illinois State University, students highlight a welcoming and diverse campus community that fosters strong connections among peers and professors . The campus has a cozy, small-town feel, making it easier for students to engage with one another and get involved in various clubs and activities. Academically, while many feel supported and appreciate the availability of helpful faculty , experiences with professors can vary significantly, with some classes being perceived as less challenging. The campus design is easy to navigate, although housing quality and dining variety are noted as areas for improvement . Overall, students enjoy their time at ISU, appreciating the educational opportunities and supportive atmosphere while recognizing some limitations in food options and facilities.

reviews.text: As long as you are a part of some club where you actively do lots of things or are in green life you will have fun and they always have events going on. However they did not cancel school when the windchill was -21 even though all the k-12 schools they own closed. Teachers still held classes in the unsafe temperatures.

Illinois State is the Harvard of the Midwest.
Professors teach a majority of the classes vs. TA’s.
Campus is extremely walkable with an emphasis on being well manicured and maintained.
Great value for the quality of education that you receive.
Getting its start as a “Normal” school to educate teachers in 1857, it has a rich history and tradition and proudly carries the title of the oldest public university in Illinois.
State Farm Hall of Business is tremendous, and no surprise they offer one of the largest Risk Management and Insurance programs in the country with Top 10 rankings through the years. The current CEO’s of Progressive and State Farm graduated from ISU. State Farm and Country Companies are headquartered in the town.
New College of Engineering will benefit from Rivian manufacturing electric vehicles in town.
Great student life with Uptown Normal right on campus with shops and restaurants. Bloomington-Normal is an extremely safe town for 130,000.
Roll Birds

I am enjoying Illinois state university. It’s so much balanced than high school was. I’m loving my classes except for French that I don’t understand and philosophy the teacher weird but other than that I’m loving it.

i think that because this school is considered a PWI it can be hard to connect with other students who may come from you same background. I do enjoy this school, from the teachers that I have had they have all been understanding and knowledgeable in what they teach.

My time at Illinois State University has been a fun and rewarding experience. I have gotten to meet many new friends and establish those lifelong connections that I can take beyond my 2 years here. I look forward to what the rest of my time at ISU will bring and I look forward to being more involved in the future.

During my time at Illinois State, the professors have been nothing but helpful and wanted to see student succeed. while not in class there is always something to do on campus with many great extracurricular activities and many options to stay active and engaged.
I like my college life on campus, with meeting new people and exploring new things . I’ve joined a club and enjoyed everything about my college experience thus far.
I think ISU has so many opportunities for its students to excel once they leave, but while still attending, your academics and constant focus on student fees occupy your mind.

I personally have had a great experience at Illinois State University. I've loved all the classes I have taken so far. I am in a lot of clubs that I love and have met amazing people in. The area is safe and easy to navigate, and I utilize all of the resources provided to me, including the free bus transportation, the campus gym, and more.
So for so good, I’m working with other college students on this Campus, my roommate is ok, and the food was ok.

I have absolutely loved my experience so far at Illinois State University. Through different clubs and groups, I have been able to find a community that is willing to help and support me through my academic career at ISU!

ISU was a great experience for a "big school / small town" vibe. It allowed students to always meet someone new but also see familiar faces on a daily basis on campus.
The wide range of class sizes kept academic life diverse with larger pre-req courses to smaller, more intimate major specific courses. Professors would always go out of their way to offer resources to help their students grow and understand the material.
Student Life consistently organized events for the student body, ranging from concerts in the Bone Cetner to festivals and fairs on the gorgeous quad. The amount of student led clubs let individuals explore their interests both inside and outside of their studies.
Overall, my experience with ISU was great. For all the amazing reasons above, I was able to work hard on my schoolwork while also gaining lifelong friendships!

As a first semester freshman at Illinois State University, I would say my experience has been life changing. This is my first time away from home and I now feel that I have two homes which I would have never expected to feel this way coming into college. I love the environment and how much the campus has to offer. It truly amazes me on how much I can do at my school. I love the recreational centers group workouts. It is a fun way to get out of the dorm and exercise all while being around your friends and laughter. I am very fond of the Milner library where I get all my studying done. I absolutely adore the campus atmosphere and the social life of the campus. One thing I would like to see change is the lack of variety at the dining hall. I love the food served, although it feels like I am eating the same meals back-to-back.

I am enjoying my first semester at ISU. It's a big campus, so getting a lot of steps in and walking around campus. I have enjoyed meeting new friends and enjoying my classes. The dorm and the food are good, and its been good so far!
This is a great institution, especially for education majors. Dorms are overcrowded, but that is my only complaint.

ai_summary: Immaculata University is recognized for its welcoming and close-knit community , which many students appreciate as it fosters strong connections with professors and peers. The small class sizes are frequently highlighted, allowing for personalized attention and support, particularly in academic settings like the nursing program, which is seen as a standout area. While the campus beauty and peaceful environment receive praise, some reviews express concerns about outdated facilities , dining options, and limited student life activities, suggesting a more vibrant social scene could enhance the experience. Additionally, there's a mixture of positive and negative feedback regarding communication and administrative support. Overall, students find a sense of belonging at Immaculata, although various areas for improvement exist.

reviews.text:
When I first started at Immaculata , I remember I was so nervous , quiet ,and scared of participating in class, everything appeared strange because I think I chose the wrong school for me . But in the last couple months I was living my best college life , I made so many friends, I started interacting in the classes that I have, having an amazing atmosphere ,and creating a good with the teachers. Personally I don’t think I would change anything at Immaculata because I like it there, the people , and the culture, Everything is so fine there.
I love my school !

I absolutely love my overall experience at Immaculata University. I love how helpful the professors are and how much they truly care and want to help you. There’s so many opportunities to get involved on campus. I don’t think anything should change because I think this campus is good the way it is.

Immaculata is a small school located in a very nice area. With this, I enjoyed my time as there was so much to do with King of Prussia being so close. I liked having these fun activities close by. I also like the close knit environment where the student professor ratio is only 11:1. I got close with my teachers and also my classmates. This gave me the chance to really enjoy the time I have had.

I transferred into immaculata University as a 42 year old male and have found the experience to exceed all expectations. The care that each and everyone has shown me is indescribable wonderful. Thank you to everyone that makes the college what it is today. I also discovered that my great grandfather used to take the Sister's from Philadelphia to the campus back in the 1940's-50's and my mother is an adjunct professor as well as a nurse at Camilla Hall.

I am a new student at Immaculata University and was nervous about becoming a part of the community. However, the students and professors have been so welcoming! At Immaculata University I do not feel like a number. It is a small school, which allows me to build better connections with my professors and opens doors to new professional connections and opportunities. I hope Immaculata can expand its student employment options to include positions that help offset tuition costs and provide more opportunities for involvement. Currently, most on-campus jobs are limited to the cafeteria, and additional roles and clubs would help students become more engaged and financially supported.

The scenery around campus is gorgeous and makes the university feel very comforting. The dorms are nice though sometimes there was some people running through the halls and kicking doors when I was in my freshman year. I just wish the campus provided transportation since there’s nothing in walking distance.

I love it. This is a place where you can instantly find friends and feel like you are home. Everyone is friendly and amicable and is willing to help you out if needed. The professors will help you if you are struggling and are willing to stay after class so that you understand the material.

Overall, I've loved my experience at Immaculata. The professors really care about you, and the classes are engaging. Clubs are a big part of campus life and there's a little something for everyone. I've met amazing people, and the area is very nice. It's close to everything while feeling somewhat removed from the chaos of busy cities and towns. I'd highly recommend checking it out!

Going to Immaculata was the best choice I have ever made. The vibe on campus is everything I was looking for when looking at schools. There are always fun events going on whether its hosted by student life, a club, etc... Academically the professors are super helpful and understanding. I am an education major and just in my second year I was already out in classrooms as well as given other opportunities to get experience. I am also an athlete and have truly loved my experience as a student athlete. I am surrounded by lots of encouragement from coaches, teammates, and the overall school community. The community at Immaculata makes the experience 10x better. I would absolutely recommend this school to anyone.

Just starting as a freshman. Would like to see a little more info on dorms to be able to plan on what to bring. (Dimensions, clearance under beds, desk size, etc)
Online on boarding is a little confusing. Would like ot see website more bulletpoints. Check it off and continue.

My experience has been great thus far. I have professors who truly care about me and my education and provide numerous ways to achieve my goals. One thing in particular I love is our Bruder Center where we can go to collect our thoughts whenever we are stressed out about anything and everything. I feel like I have learned some much about the world of music and I can’t wait to continue learning more.

I decided to go back to get a second Bachelor’s. This University has exceeded my expectations. The professors are helpful and give you the resources you need to succeed. The campus is not only beautiful but it’s extremely safe to walk on even after night courses. I am impressed and very proud to be a part of this University.

Immaculata university is a Catholic university, with diversity and appreciates values of people from various cultures. The professors are proactive, always willing to support students to ensure they achieve their goals. I like the communal life where everyone feels like a member of a great family of Immaculata university. However, if ticketing of people’s cars can be reduced, it will ease socialization within the school, to welcome both students and visitors.

If you cannot get a flu shot or a covid shot (even if you have allergies), you will NOT be successful in any of their nurse degree programs, but they won’t tell you that and will instead string you along with the hope that you may be placed in a clinical. I was forced to drop out one day before the accelerated cohort of 2025 was to begin because I still had not been given a clinical placement-yet the clinical coordinator never communicated to me that she simply had no placement for me. Clinical placements must be taken every semester in the accelerated program because they necessitate graduation. I had to take action in dropping my classes 1 day before they would begin to get a full refund, otherwise I would have paid $10,000 for the first semester-money down the drain. So, I would NOT recommend Immaculata University because the communication is very poor and they are not truthful that you WILL NOT be successful in any of their nursing degree programs without a covid and flu shot.

Immaculata University creates a warm and enabling environment for students to achieve optimum academic excellence. The professors are understanding and experienced, showing eagerness to support students at all times. In addition, the administrative staffs support students to ensure that all students have the right strategy focused towards achieving their goals. Indeed, Immaculata university is a place to be. Social life is strikingly exciting, as the school considers athletics important. Overall, Immaculata university is an excellent catholic university, with experienced professors and administration, focused at supporting all students achieve their best academic goals.

ai_summary: At Indiana State University, students generally appreciate the close-knit community and accessibility of faculty, with many noting that professors are approachable and genuinely care about student success. Reviews highlight the advantages of smaller class sizes that foster personal relationships with instructors, and there are numerous opportunities for involvement through clubs and activities. However, concerns are raised about campus safety, food quality, and the responsiveness of the financial aid office, which some believe could be improved. The university is recognized for its diverse environment and strong academic programs , especially in areas like nursing and criminology, but some students express frustration over administrative inefficiencies and limited support for specific needs. Overall, many find Indiana State to be a welcoming and supportive place, despite some challenges.

reviews.text: Indiana State University has been my home away from home. I feel safe being here, and honestly, my dorm feels more like home than home anymore. Great people here, and it’s been a great stepping stone in my life.

My overall experience at Indiana State University has been positive and a big learning experience so far. As a freshman studying psychology and criminology, I’ve appreciated the supportive learning environment and the opportunity to begin exploring subjects that interest me. The campus community has been welcoming, and my classes have helped me start building a strong foundation for my future career goals.
